item.title: 中文新闻文本TTS基准测试与评估
item.content:encoded:
中文新闻TTS系统在处理复杂文本时表现不一，最佳系统准确率达87.9%。



摘要： 本文介绍了CN-NewsTTS Bench v0.1，一个用于评估中文新闻文本语音合成系统是否能正确发音的开放基准。该基准包含200条开发集、800条测试集、992个可自动评估的目标，以及基于三ASR集成的固定转录文本和自动评分工具。研究还提供了ASR路线诊断、子集消融实验、类别结果、置信区间和供应商配置元数据。测试结果显示，最佳系统严格准确率为0.879，而部分系统仍低于0.60。

item.title: 大型语言模型社会偏见评估的框架与发现
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示比较设置加剧模型潜在偏见，提出统一评估框架。



摘要： 随着大型语言模型在关键应用中的广泛部署，评估其社会偏见变得尤为重要。然而，现有研究方法存在碎片化问题，导致结论矛盾。本文提出一个统一且可控的框架，标准化不同基准以系统对比孤立评估与强制选择比较设置。该框架能够分离链式推理、中性备选方案等结构因素对偏见评估的影响。实验表明，比较设置显著激活了模型的潜在歧视，而链式推理进一步加剧了这一现象。即使提供中性选项或声称随机回答，系统性偏见依然存在。研究还发现，这种比较偏见随模型规模增加而增强，强调在实际应用中需谨慎使用比较设置。

item.title: 基于TIGER框架的高质量人脸视频修复方法
item.content:encoded:
TIGER框架提升人脸视频修复的准确性与一致性。



摘要： 本文提出TIGER框架，通过融合身份、几何和生成先验来解决人脸视频修复中的三大挑战：身份漂移、视角纠缠和感知真实感。该框架首先在潜在空间中注入具有辨识度的身份嵌入以保持身份一致性；其次通过将2D参考线索提升到解耦的3D参数空间构建几何先验，确保动态视频结构稳定；最后利用视频生成模型的生成先验实现高效修复。此外，研究还设计了三阶段训练优化策略，提升结构保真度、纹理重建和分布级真实感，并构建了大规模FVR数据集用于训练和评估。实验表明，TIGER在身份保真度和时间稳定性方面均达到领先水平。

item.title: NavWM：一种统一的导航世界模型
item.content:encoded:
NavWM提升复杂环境下的导航性能。



摘要： 本文提出NavWM，一种统一的导航世界模型，整合了潜在世界推理、多模态动作预测和可控视觉生成。通过使用潜在世界标记提取几何和语义先验，NavWM增强了代理的结构理解能力。引入基于锚点的多模式轨迹预测框架，解决了确定性策略的局限性，提升了生成世界的鲁棒性。实验表明，NavWM在高保真未来状态生成和零样本导航成功率方面均有显著提升。

item.title: 批量实体解析的优化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究如何高效地通过有限批次的实体解析实现大规模数据的完整实体识别。



摘要： 本文探讨了在每次只能处理有限批次记录的条件下，如何通过查询实体解析器来解决大规模数据集中的实体关联问题。研究提出了一种按需处理的方法，在控制查询成本的同时尽可能提高召回率。作者将该问题形式化为批量实体解析，并证明了最优批次选择是NP难的。在特定实体大小假设下，提出了一个最优解。最后，通过六个数据集的实验验证了该方法优于现有最佳方法。

item.title: Agora基准测试：跨文档推理与档案探索的挑战
item.content:encoded:
Agora基准测试评估大模型在跨文档推理和档案探索中的表现。



摘要： 本文介绍了Agora，一个用于评估大语言模型在跨文档推理和档案探索能力的新基准。该基准包含362个问题，覆盖8个领域的9,664份真实文档，总词数达3.72亿。由于数据量远超模型的上下文窗口，模型必须进行有策略的探索而非全面扫描。研究发现，即使最先进的模型在该任务上的准确率也仅达到59.4%，且不同领域表现差异显著。

item.title: Ariadne：基于提示的分子合成路线生成模型
item.content:encoded:
Ariadne通过提示生成实现高效合成路线规划，提升性能。



摘要： Ariadne是一种基于解码器的分子合成路线生成模型，能够通过单一提示序列同时处理目标分子、约束条件和合成路线。在RetroCast/PaRoutes基准测试中，Ariadne在深度约束和所需起始材料任务上分别提升了13.7和31.2个点。相比DES P，Ariadne在24 GPU分钟内实现了更好的Top-10和Solv-0表现。尽管在某些基准上仍不及AiZynthFinder MCTS，但Ariadne在路线保留重建任务中表现出色。研究团队发布了代码和训练脚本以支持后续研究，但未引入Tier-1至Tier-3路线检查器，这仍是该类模型应用于实验化学家的主要瓶颈。

item.title: 基于LLM的事实核查系统改进方法
item.content:encoded:
SIFT提升事实核查系统准确性与证据有效性。



摘要： 本文提出SIFT方法，通过重新评分提取的证据片段以提高事实核查系统的准确性。结合WSP指标，评估引用证据是否有效支持主张。在多个基准数据集上测试，SIFT显著提升准确率，并优于直接提示方法。WSP在人类标注数据上表现出高AUC和精确度，证明其有效性。

item.title: 基于神经网络的耦合遍历倒向随机微分方程数值解法
item.content:encoded:
提出两种神经网络方法求解耦合遍历倒向随机微分方程，用于优化策略近似。



摘要： 本文介绍了两种基于神经网络的数值方案，用于求解耦合的遍历倒向随机微分方程（eBSDEs），旨在优化在制度切换随机因子模型中的前向效用函数下的最优策略。该方法基于文献[HLT20]中提出的模型表示，并通过将eBSDE的解与具有随机终端时间的多维BSDE联系起来进行构建。文中引入了一种局部加法深度学习方案，通过最小化局部误差项来实现。此外，还提出了一种受Deep Galerkin Method启发的算法，通过最小化相关遍历PDE系统的残差来求解。最后，将该框架应用于制度切换前向效用函数的随机因子模型，通过数值实验验证了方法的有效性，并分析了制度切换对前向偏好影响。

item.title: 基于结构化对象信息的室内视觉重定位系统OpenReLoc
item.content:encoded:
提出OpenReLoc系统提升室内视觉重定位精度与场景理解能力。



摘要： 本文针对室内视觉重定位问题，提出OpenReLoc系统，通过整合语义、布局和几何信息构建结构化地图，利用对象单元驱动相机重定位。系统引入多模态机制进行2D-3D对象匹配，并设计基于Distance-IoU的参考帧选择策略以支持大规模场景。同时，采用双路径2D迭代最近像素损失优化姿态，实验表明该方法在多个数据集上均取得优异性能。

item.title: 基于PTv3的医学点云补全方法PCFM研究
item.content:encoded:
PCFM在医学点云补全中表现优异，速度快且效果好。



摘要： 本文研究了医学点云补全问题，提出了一种基于PTv3的流匹配方法PCFM。通过在SkullFix、SkullBreak和Mandibular Defect数据集上的实验，验证了PCFM的有效性。与传统扩散模型相比，PCFM在保持高质量生成的同时，显著减少了采样步骤，提升了处理速度。此外，研究还探讨了模型规模和点数对性能的影响，展示了高分辨率点云带来的性能提升。

item.title: 基于梯度的视觉-语言大模型审计框架GradAudit
item.content:encoded:
GradAudit通过分析梯度信号检测VLLM训练数据，提升版权审计准确性。



摘要： 本文提出GradAudit，一种基于梯度的视觉-语言大模型审计框架。该方法通过分析模型在训练过程中的内部优化动态，而非依赖外部输出信号，有效区分训练样本与非训练样本。实验表明，GradAudit在医疗和通用领域数据集上均优于现有方法，能够准确检测真实图像-文本关联，而非仅依赖单一模态信息。研究还指出，现有方法低估了未经授权数据的使用范围，且随着模型进步，这种低估更加明显。

item.title: 因果推理与强化学习的融合：因果强化学习的提出
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨因果推理与强化学习的结合，提出因果强化学习新范式。



摘要： 文章指出因果推理和强化学习虽然独立发展，但都涉及反事实关系，具有内在联系。通过将环境建模为结构因果模型，可以统一处理在线学习、离线策略学习和因果计算等不同模式。文章进一步引入广义策略学习、模仿学习和反事实学习等新任务，推动了因果推理与强化学习的协同研究，提出因果强化学习（CRL）的概念，揭示其广阔的应用前景。

item.title: 高效图像编辑工具EPEdit提升用户操作体验
item.content:encoded:
EPEdit提供高效、易用的图像编辑功能，无需专业技能。



摘要： 随着图像编辑需求的增长，传统工具如Photoshop和Capture One虽强大但操作复杂。新兴的AI平台如Luminar Neo、Pixlr X和Canva虽然提供了更多选择，但往往需要大量训练和调整，成本较高。为此，EPEdit应运而生，它结合了强大的后端框架和直观的前端界面，支持多种图像编辑任务，包括对象替换、背景修改、视角调整等。EPEdit基于Stable Diffusion模型，采用零样本图像编辑算法，无需额外微调，使用户能通过简单文本指令或标记区域进行精确编辑，降低了使用门槛。测试结果显示，EPEdit在图像编辑、主题设计和系统性能方面均优于现有解决方案，是一款高效且经济的图像处理工具。

item.title: 基于CORE-BREW的多比特水印技术提升LLM输出可信溯源
item.content:encoded:
CORE-BREW提高LLM输出水印的鲁棒性和低误报率。



摘要： 本文提出CORE-BREW，一种改进的多比特水印方法，用于增强大语言模型（LLM）输出的可信溯源。该方法通过固定命中率校准水印信道，生成闭式每标记对数似然比，支持严格安全和误报率校准两种检测模式。实验表明，CORE-BREW在令牌级编辑和改写情况下，相比现有方法具有更好的低误报率和鲁棒性，同时保持相似的语义质量。

item.title: 基于ESPINN的相场模型逆向识别方法研究
item.content:encoded:
ESPINN框架可高效逆向识别相场模型参数。



摘要： 本文提出了一种扩展伪谱物理信息神经网络（ESPINN）框架，用于从瞬时快照数据中逆向识别相场模型。该方法能够同时恢复体化学势和未知梯度系数，在无噪声情况下表现出高精度和统计稳定性，即使仅使用单对快照也能恢复大量结构信息。在有噪声的情况下，重建精度逐渐下降，但增加快照数量可提高鲁棒性。该方法为相分离连续模型中的自由能结构学习提供了一种数据高效且物理一致的解决方案。

item.title: 基于变分自编码器的车辆气动模拟模型降阶研究
item.content:encoded:
研究通过变分自编码器提升气动模拟模型的降阶效果。



摘要： 本文探讨了在工业应用中通过模型降阶技术减少计算资源消耗的方法，特别是在车辆气动设计领域。研究结合神经网络与变分自编码器，对高雷诺数下的多车型气流进行高效建模和重构，重点评估了不同空间和时间尺度下涡旋生成的重建精度，尤其关注车辆尾部流场行为。该方法旨在提高模拟效率并保持高精度。

item.title: 面向艺术文字的场景文本识别方法与数据集构建
item.content:encoded:
提出WATER-S数据集和WATERec模型提升艺术文字识别性能。



摘要： 本文针对艺术文字场景文本识别（WATER）的挑战，提出了一个大规模合成数据集WATER-S，包含两个互补子集，分别通过升级渲染引擎和结合Qwen3-VL与Z-Image生成高质量数据。同时，设计了WATERec模型，采用支持任意形状输入的视觉编码器和自回归解码器，突破传统固定模板的限制。实验表明，该方法在WordArt-Bench上达到90.40%的准确率，显著优于现有方法。

item.title: 无需训练的跨域少样本分割方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需训练的跨域少样本分割框架，提升分割性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种无需训练的跨域少样本分割方法，旨在解决传统方法依赖训练过程导致的高计算成本和过拟合问题。该方法基于自监督视觉编码器DINOv3，通过三个核心模块：语义感知特征重融合（SAFR）、自适应支持增强（ASE）和混合原型匹配（HPM），提升模型在不同目标域中的泛化能力。实验表明，该方法在四个目标域数据集上均取得最优性能，且无需任何训练。

item.title: 基于时间重参数化与累积强度外推的离散流匹配采样方法
item.content:encoded:
提出TR-CIE采样器提升有限函数评估下的离散流匹配质量。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Time-Reparameterized Cumulative Intensity Extrapolation (TR-CIE)的新型采样方法，用于改进离散流匹配（DFM）在有限函数评估次数下的采样质量。该方法包含两个核心组件：一是基于时间重参数化的调度调整，通过重新缩放时间网格来缓解终端采样阶段的刚性问题；二是累积强度外推更新规则，利用前一步的模型输出作为历史项以提高非均匀时间网格上的累积强度近似效果。理论分析表明该方法能有效控制局部误差并保证收敛性，且每步仅需一次函数评估，无需额外计算。实验结果表明，在合成任务、文本生成和图文生成基准上，该方法在有限NFE下显著提升了采样质量。

item.title: ComputeFHE：基于TFHE的隐私保护计算库
item.content:encoded:
ComputeFHE提升加密数据计算效率，降低开发复杂度。



摘要： 本文介绍了ComputeFHE，一个基于TFHE密码系统的开源C++库，旨在简化隐私保护应用的开发。该库支持加密整数和定点数据类型，并提供算术、逻辑、比较、条件和无感知数组访问操作，使开发者能以熟悉的命令式编程方式实现算法。ComputeFHE采用标准逻辑门和优化的ALU架构，显著减少所需的刷新操作，提升性能达3.9倍。此外，它还包含模拟模式，可在不执行实际加密计算的情况下进行测试与分析。ComputeFHE建立在OpenFHE之上，为隐私保护算法和应用提供了实用且易用的框架。

item.title: S1-Omni-Image：科学图像理解、生成与编辑的统一模型
item.content:encoded:
S1-Omni-Image是用于科学图像生成与编辑的统一模型，具备高精度和科学语义理解能力。



摘要： S1-Omni-Image是一款面向科学图像理解、生成与编辑的统一多模态模型。它基于S1-VL-32B科学多模态推理框架，结合图像生成模块，在生成前进行任务导向的推理，以确保科学语义的准确性。该模型支持科学插图、数据图表等生成任务，并可进行医学和地理图像分割、翻译及超分辨率处理。研究团队构建了SciGenEdit数据集，并公开了模型权重和代码。实验表明，S1-Omni-Image在多个基准测试中表现优异，优于现有开源模型。

item.title: 三维场景补全技术发展与未来方向
item.content:encoded:
综述过去十年三维场景补全技术的演进与挑战。



摘要： 本文系统回顾了2016至2026年间三维场景补全领域的研究进展，从早期基于体素语义完成的SSCNet模型，到结合生成扩散先验和实时渲染的高斯点云技术，全面分析了多种表示范式的发展，包括体素网格、点学习、隐式神经场、Transformer网络、扩散网络等。文章还构建了一个分类体系，梳理了该领域的主要贡献，并探讨了当前仍需解决的挑战，最后提出了下一代系统的研发方向。

item.title: ZONOS2 8B：最新文本转语音模型的性能提升与发布
item.content:encoded:
ZONOS2 8B在自然度、韵律和语音克隆上取得突破。



摘要： ZONOS2 8B是最新推出的文本转语音（TTS）模型，相比之前的版本在规模、数据和训练方法上均有改进。该模型参数从1.6B扩展到8B，采用新型专家混合（MoE）架构，提升了推理速度和吞吐量。训练数据也从200K小时扩展至超过6M小时，并优化了后训练和条件生成流程，以提高自然度和语音克隆的准确性。评估结果显示，ZONOS2 8B在多个基准测试中表现优异，同时保持良好的实时延迟。模型权重和示例代码已通过Apache 2.0许可证在GitHub和Hugging Face上发布。

item.title: MedBench v5：面向临床多模态模型的动态评估基准
item.content:encoded:
MedBench v5 提升了医疗AI评估的流程透明度与错误检测能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了 MedBench v5，一个针对临床多模态模型（包括语言、视觉语言和代理系统）的新一代评估基准。该基准从静态问答转向动态过程评估，包含双维框架、信息流压力测试、动态过程审计和幻觉传播监控等功能，能够更全面地评估模型在医疗场景中的表现和稳定性。实验表明，强任务性能不等于过程稳定，MedBench v5 为临床AI的评估提供了统一的基础设施。

item.title: Themis：结合可解释性和人类反馈的强化学习测试框架
item.content:encoded:
Themis是一个结合XAI和人类反馈的RL测试框架，提升安全性和性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了Themis，一个结合可解释性人工智能（XAI）和人类反馈的强化学习（RL）测试与评估框架。Themis支持超过200个常用环境，能够用于RL、透明度和对齐性实验。研究结果表明，Themis可以训练出与环境真实奖励信号相当或更优的奖励模型。此外，该框架还提供了一个基于云的平台，用于收集人类反馈并管理实验，具备用户友好、自动扩展的特点，能够在普通商用设备上支持千人同时进行实验。

item.title: 生成式AI代码代理在开源供应链中的检测与分析
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示生成式AI代码代理在开源项目中的广泛存在与检测挑战。



摘要： 本文提出了一种多层检测框架，通过配置文件扫描、提交信息分析、作者身份匹配和机器人签名查找，识别生成式AI代码代理的活动。研究发现，单一方法仅能捕捉部分活动，而多方法检测揭示了大量Claude Code提交，其中仅有3.3%被传统信号识别，表明现有估计低估了AI代理的使用情况。研究还显示，不同检测渠道捕获的代理群体差异显著，且工作类型与部署方式相关，而非工具本身。

item.title: 基于梯度的大型语言模型幻觉检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Grad Detect方法，通过分析梯度模式检测LLM幻觉。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为Grad Detect的基于梯度的方法，用于通过分析推理过程中的层级梯度模式来预测大型语言模型（LLMs）的幻觉。研究表明，模型内部的梯度结构包含了关于输出正确性的丰富信息，这些信息无法仅通过输出信号获得。在多个问答基准测试中，该方法在幻觉检测和模型拒绝预测方面均优于基于置信度和采样的基线方法。通过全面的层消融实验，发现最后五层集中了超过97%的判别梯度信号，使得部署更加高效且性能损失最小。Grad Detect提供了一个统一的框架，用于预测LLM可靠性多个维度，并提供了可解释的模型失败原因分析。

item.title: DL-Lite中理性闭包的推理与查询回答研究
item.content:encoded:
研究DL-Lite中理性闭包的推理与查询效率。



摘要： 本文探讨了在DL-Lite轻量描述逻辑家族的核心和Horn变体中应用理性闭包（RC）的推理方法。研究重点包括实例检查和联合查询（CQ）回答，提出了一种基于现有标准古典推理机的插件架构，证明了在DL-Lite中使用RC进行推理和查询回答可以高效实现且计算开销最小。

item.title: 基于概率框架的阿尔茨海默病进展建模方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种概率框架用于预测阿尔茨海默病进展及不确定性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种结合有序诊断预测、多时间点轨迹生成和分解不确定性估计的概率框架，用于更准确地描述阿尔茨海默病患者的长期病情演变。通过改进的时序融合变压器编码器和混合密度网络，模型能够生成五年内的诊断状态、CDR评分、MMSE定向能力和海马体积等概率轨迹。实验表明该模型在MCI与痴呆区分上表现优异，并能有效分离随机不确定性和知识性不确定性，为临床提供更可靠的预测依据。

item.title: Exploring the relationship between human-centric AI and firm idiosyncratic risks
item.content:encoded: Despite the extensive discussions of human-centric AI (HCAI) in Industry 5.0, its effects on firms' idiosyncratic risks (IR) remains underexplored. This is an imperative issue for firms navigate financial risks during the current technological revolution, as IR reflects investor reactions to corporate heterogeneous AI strategies and implementations by isolating firm-level stock volatility from systematic factors. Integrating situated AI theory with social-technical systems theory, we conceptualise HCAI as a situated AI strategy that reduces AI-related ethical risks and fosters AI-Human synergies in firms' business operations, ultimately reducing IR by aligning with stakeholders' diverse expectations. Moreover, socio-technical factors, namely digitalisation, operational efficiency, executive shareholding, and CEOs with IT background, may moderate the HCAI-IR relationship. Using a multi-source panel dataset of Chinese listed firms from 2015 to 2023, we find that HCAI is associated with lower firm IR. Furthermore, digitalisation and executive shareholding strengthen this risk-reducing effect, whereas operational efficiency and CEOs with IT background surprisingly attenuate it. Our findings offer theoretical contributions and practical insights for both ethical AI governance and firm financial risk management in the AI era.

item.title: CompressKV: Semantic-Retrieval-Guided KV-Cache Compression for Resource-Efficient Long-Context LLM Inference
item.content:encoded: Long-context large language model (LLM) inference is increasingly constrained by the memory footprint and decoding cost of key-value (KV) caches, limiting sustainable deployment on resource-constrained hardware. Existing KV cache eviction methods typically apply heuristic token scoring over all heads in GQA-based LLMs. These methods ignore the different functionalities of attention heads, leading to the eviction of critical tokens and thus degrading the performance of LLMs. To address this issue, we propose CompressKV, a resource-efficient KV-cache compression framework for GQA-based LLMs. Instead of aggregating attention scores from all heads, CompressKV identifies Semantic Retrieval Heads (SRHs) that capture both the initial and final tokens of a prompt and semantically important mid-context evidence, and uses them to select tokens whose KV pairs should be retained. Furthermore, CompressKV allocates cache budgets across layers according to offline estimates of layer-wise eviction error. Experiments on LongBench and Needle-in-a-Haystack show that CompressKV consistently outperforms existing KV-cache eviction methods across memory budgets. Notably, it preserves over 97\% of full-cache performance using only 3\% of the KV cache on LongBench question-answering tasks and achieves 90\% accuracy with just 0.7\% KV storage on Needle-in-a-Haystack. These results demonstrate an improved resource--performance trade-off for long-context LLM inference. Our code is publicly available at: https://github.com/TUDa-HWAI/CompressKV

item.title: Red-Teaming the Agentic Red-Team
item.content:encoded: The use of agentic systems to perform offensive security operations has moved from a theoretical possibility to a commoditized capability. However, while the community has focused on creating more and more capable agents, less attention has been allocated to assessing the security of those systems. In this work, we present the first in-depth security analysis of the most widely used agentic systems for offensive security operations. We show that most of these tools share common design flaws that enable an active adversary to exfiltrate API keys, establish persistent footholds, and fully compromise the operator's machine, even when the agent operates inside a sandboxed container. To support our analysis, we introduce a full cyber kill chain for such agentic systems, capturing the progression from initial LLM manipulation to lateral movement, persistence, guardrail bypass, and sandbox escape. Building on our security analysis, we derive a robust architecture for agentic offensive-security tools and propose actionable, broadly applicable design principles that mitigate the disclosed attack paths at the architectural level.

item.title: UniDrive: A Unified Vision-Language and Grounding Framework for Interpretable Risk Understanding in Autonomous Driving
item.content:encoded: Recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have shown strong potential for autonomous driving scene understanding, yet existing methods still face a fundamental trade-off between temporal reasoning and spatial precision. Models that rely on single-frame or low-resolution inputs often miss small, distant, or partially occluded hazards, while language-centric driving models frequently provide limited grounded evidence for their explanations. To address this gap, we propose UniDrive, a unified visual-language and grounding framework for interpretable risk understanding in autonomous driving. UniDrive combines a temporal reasoning branch that models scene dynamics from multi-frame visual input with a high-resolution perception branch that preserves fine-grained spatial details from the latest frame. The two branches are integrated through a gated cross-attention fusion module, enabling dynamic context to be aligned with precise spatial evidence. Based on the fused representation, UniDrive jointly generates natural-language risk descriptions and grounded bounding-box outputs for risk objects. Experiments on the DRAMA-Reasoning benchmark show that UniDrive outperforms representative image-based and video-based baselines in both captioning and risk-object grounding. In particular, UniDrive achieves the best overall performance on the validation split and demonstrates clear advantages in small-object localization, zero-shot generalization to NuScenes and BDD100K, and human-rated interpretability and trustworthiness. These results suggest that explicitly combining temporal semantics and high-resolution perception provides a stronger foundation for interpretable and safety-oriented autonomous driving systems. The code is available at https://github.com/pixeli99/unidrive-dev.

item.title: ReM-MoA: Reasoning Memory Sustains Mixture-of-Agents Scaling
item.content:encoded: Mixture-of-Agents (MoA) architectures improve inference-time scaling by organizing multiple LLM agents into layered reasoning pipelines. However, existing MoA variants fail to sustain gains as depth increases, exhibiting degradation, early plateauing, or saturation. We propose ReM-MoA, a memory-augmented MoA framework that sustains scaling through two mechanisms: (1) a Ranked Reasoning Memory that persistently stores and ranks reasoning traces from all layers using a comparative Reviewer Agent, and (2) a Curated Diversified Memory Routing scheme that exposes different agents to distinct combinations of successful and failed traces, preserving exploration diversity while propagating high-quality reasoning. We further introduce an optional multi-domain Reviewer distillation pipeline that improves ranking quality through frontier-model supervision. Across five reasoning benchmarks spanning math, formal logic, code, knowledge, and commonsense, ReM-MoA consistently outperforms prior MoA variants across both depth and width scaling, and its advantage widens with depth, establishing structured cross-layer reasoning memory as a key missing mechanism for scalable multi-agent inference.

item.title: Accelerating Disaggregated RL for Visual Generative LLMs with Diffusion-Based Parallelism and Trainer-Assisted Generation
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a dominant post-training paradigm, driving the emergence of high-performance RL systems such as veRL for autoregressive large language models (LLMs). In parallel, diffusion-oriented RL algorithms, e.g., DanceGRPO and FlowGRPO, have rapidly expanded the scope of RL from language reasoning to diffusion-based visual and flow-based generation. However, efficient RL systems for diffusion generative LLMs remain underexplored. Existing implementations, e.g., veRL-Omni, still rely on colocated execution, which simplifies synchronization but couples rollout and training resources, limits heterogeneous deployment, and constrains independent scaling. To this end, we introduce DigenRL, a disaggregated RL framework for diffusion-based generative LLMs that supports flexible resource allocation, accommodates heterogeneous GPUs, and facilitates efficient task scheduling. To maximally reduce the execution bubbles in the disaggregated architecture, we propose: 1) a generation-axis pipeline (GAP) and time-step parallelism (TSP) in the diffusion architecture to enable finer-grained pipelining between rollout and training; 2) an elastic trainer-assisted generation (TAG) approach to enable the trainer GPU resources to dynamically assist in executing rollout generations; and 3) a tightly one-step constrained asynchronous strategy to further utilize the tail bubble in the pipeline. Extensive experiments are conducted on three hardware testbeds with 16-32 GPUs using HunyuanVideo-13B, Wan2.1-14B, FLUX.1-12B, and QwenImage-20B generative models. Experimental results show that DigenRL achieves 1.56-2.10x throughput improvements over state-of-the-art diffusion RL systems, veRL-Omni and GenRL.

item.title: Prob-BBDM: a Probabilistic Brownian Bridge Diffusion Model for MRI sequence image-to-image translation
item.content:encoded: AI-driven image-to-image synthesis is rapidly advancing, with growing applications in medical imaging. Multi-modal image analysis plays a crucial role in optimizing examination quality, yet acquiring multiple imaging modalities in clinical settings remains resource-intensive and time-consuming, especially for 3D imaging. To address this challenge, we propose a novel image-to-image translation model based on Brownian Bridge Diffusion Models (BBDM), which synthesizes magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) sequences from 2D axial slices. Our approach integrates a variational encoder-guided diffusion mechanism, leveraging probabilistic image distributions to enhance synthesis quality. Evaluated on the BraTS 2021 dataset, our Probabilistic-BBDM (Prob-BBDM) achieves superior performance across multiple translation tasks, reaching up to 88.46% SSIM and 26.09 dB PSNR, with consistent improvements over baselines. Notably, our diffusion process requires only 4 steps, making it computationally efficient while maintaining high-quality synthesis. To further validate generalizability, we test Prob-BBDM on an external third-party dataset, demonstrating consistent performance across domains. Additionally, we assess the clinical utility of the synthesized slices by using them as input to a pre-trained segmentation model. Tumor segmentation yields a Dice score of 88.71% and an HD95 of 3.49 mm, confirming that the synthesized slices preserve critical diagnostic information. These results highlight the potential of Prob-BBDM for high-quality, efficient, and generalizable MRI synthesis, offering a promising step toward improved medical image translation.

item.title: Less is More: Quality-Aware Training Data Selection for Scientific Summarization
item.content:encoded: Scientific long-document summarization datasets commonly treat author-written abstracts as gold reference summaries, although their quality and alignment with the source article vary. At the same time, publicly available scientific summarization datasets remain limited in scale and structure for modern long-context models. In this work, we address both challenges by a) constructing and releasing one of the largest biomedical and life science datasets for long-document summarization, containing 1.88 million PMC articles, and b) analyzing the reference quality of author-written abstracts with source-grounded and model-based metrics. We show that author-written abstracts vary in their alignment with the full article and that these quality signals can guide training-data selection. Training on selected high-quality subsets outperforms random sampling at matched training sizes and can match or exceed larger random subsets on factuality-oriented metrics. Our findings suggest that reference quality is an important factor in scientific summarization and that quality-aware data selection can improve training efficiency.

item.title: Infinitesimal Causality
item.content:encoded: This paper introduces a categorical account of infinitesimal causality in Frobenius Markov categories equipped with tangent-bundle semantics. IDC captures the infinitesimal layer in which interventions act as tangent deformations of copy/discard structure. Two distinct Frobenius structures interact: (1) the categorical Frobenius algebra on classical variables encoding copying, comparing, and discarding; and (2) the geometric Frobenius integrability condition, namely involutive closure of the intervention distribution, distinct from the algebraic Frobenius structure. Categorical causal sufficiency is defined as the compatibility of these two notions. A key observation is that, for structural causal models, infinitesimal causality is most naturally formulated in the slice of deterministic mechanisms over exogenous variables, with visible stochastic kernels obtained only after pushforward. Interventions are tangent vectors that deform the Frobenius copy/discard operations; their Lie brackets measure whether this deformation preserves classical information-flow structure. Pearl's do-calculus is used as a guiding example of intervention identities: ignoring irrelevant interventions corresponds to counit invariance, action/observation exchange to coproduct compatibility with pushforward, and independence to involutive bracket closure of the visible intervention distribution.

item.title: PointVG-R: Internalizing Geometric Reasoning in MLLMs for Precise Pointing Localization via Visual Chain of Thought
item.content:encoded: Pointing-based visual grounding requires models to precisely locate target objects by deciphering complex spatial relationships between the visual scene and pointing gestures. Traditional methods typically encode input images into static feature representations and perform reasoning primarily within the linguistic domain, often overlooking the rich perceptual cues and explicit spatial geometry inherent in images. In this study, we aim to mitigate the cognitive vulnerability of models in interpreting gestural spatial relations by proposing PointVG-R, a reasoning-guided Multi-modal Large Language Model (MLLM). PointVG-R introduces geometric-aware reasoning for pointing-based grounding, enabling the model to think with images through the strategic integration of Reinforcement Learning (RL) and cold-start data. Specifically, we design a novel geometric reasoning pipeline that simulates the iterative cognitive process humans employ when interpreting pointing gestures. Furthermore, we construct EgoPoint-CoT, a high-quality visual Chain-of-Thought (CoT) dataset featuring detailed reasoning trajectories to guide the model via Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and RL. To address the varying quality of learning signals encountered during training, we further propose an Adaptive Importance Weighting strategy based on Group Variance, which dynamically adjusts reward signals to optimize the learning process. Experimental results demonstrate that PointVG-R achieves SOTA performance, outperforming the baseline by $\textbf{15.86}$ points in mIoU. Extensive ablation studies further validate the efficacy of our proposed modules. Code: https://github.com/lingli1724/PointVG-R.

item.title: Escaping the Self-Confirmation Trap: An Execute-Distill-Verify Paradigm for Agentic Experience Learning
item.content:encoded: Experience-driven self-evolution is critical for large language model (LLM) agents to improve through open-world interaction. However, existing experience learning methods mostly rely on single-agent loops, where the same agent executes tasks, summarizes outcomes, and determines memory content. This setup makes agents vulnerable to the Self-Confirmation Trap: wrong-but-self-consistent trajectories are misidentified as successful experience, leading to cumulative errors during retrieval and reuse. To address this issue, we propose EDV, an Execute-Distill-Verify framework for reliable experience learning. In the Execute stage, multiple heterogeneous agents explore the same task space in parallel to generate diverse candidate trajectories. In the Distill stage, a dedicated third-party agent comparatively analyzes these trajectories to produce candidate experiences, reducing executor-centric summarization bias. In the Verify stage, the execution group validates candidates via a consensus mechanism, and only approved experiences are written into shared or private memory. By decoupling the three stages, EDV transforms experience learning from isolated self-reflection into collaborative construction, filtering erroneous and noisy content before memory insertion. We evaluate EDV on three challenging long-horizon benchmarks: tau2-bench, Mind2Web and MMTB. Results show EDV consistently outperforms strong baselines, validating that reliable experience construction is essential for robust agent self-evolution. Our code is available at https://github.com/shidingz/EDV.

item.title: Cycle-Consistent Neural Explanation of Formal Verification Certificates
item.content:encoded: Formal verification produces machine-checkable certificates that attest to the satisfaction or violation of temporal properties, yet these certificates remain opaque to non-specialist stakeholders. We propose a cycle-consistent neural architecture that generates faithful natural language explanations of verification certificates. A forward network NN1 maps certificates to explanations, and an inverse network NN2 reconstructs certificates from explanations; a symbolic verifier closes the loop, providing a differentiable faithfulness proxy. A pointer-generator mechanism ensures lexical grounding by copying state names directly from the certificate. We evaluate on 420 test certificates spanning six verification methods (bounded proof, k-induction, inductive invariant, lasso, reachability, witness pair) in both YES and NO verdict variants, drawn from a financial compliance domain with 207 named states. Our trained architecture, combined with a hybrid inference-time routing strategy, achieves 90.0% cycle-verified soundness, surpassing a multi- LLM few-shot baseline (76.1% for the best of 16 LLM combinations across four frontier models) by 13.9 percentage points. The neural model wins on 10 of 12 verdict/kind categories, with three categories reaching 100% soundness. The architecture offers 860x faster inference (185 ms vs. 160 s per certificate for the full multi-LLM baseline), offline operation, deterministic outputs, and zero per-inference cost. These results demonstrate that trained specialization outperforms general-purpose LLM prompting for structured certificate explanation, while eliminating the deployment constraints of cloud-based inference.

item.title: Structural Kolmogorov-Arnold Convolutions: Learnable Function on the Values or the Filter Shape as Parameter-Efficient Alternative to Per-Edge Convolutional KANs
item.content:encoded: Convolutional Kolmogorov--Arnold Networks (KANs) replace the fixed weights of a convolutional kernel with learnable univariate functions. The dominant formulation attaches one such function to every kernel entry and lets it act on pixel values, expressive but parameter-heavy and prone to overfitting. We argue that the learnable functions are better placed in the \emph{structure} of the convolution than on each edge, and we organise the design space along a single axis: whether the function acts on the pixel \emph{values} or on the filter \emph{shape}. We study three realisations. SV-KAN applies one shared univariate function to the values and leaves the spatial filter free and static, aa classical convolution with a single learnable shared activation. AG-KAN keeps the shared value function but supplies the spatial structure through a content-adaptive Gaussian gate. RF-KAN instead moves the learnable functions onto the filter shape, building each filter from oriented ridge profiles expanded in a localised oscillatory (Morlet) wavelet basis with content-adaptive amplitudes. Under a matched four-layer protocol with in-run references and three seeds, RF-KAN and SV-KAN reach $88.47\pm0.10\%$ and $88.20\pm0.31\%$ on CIFAR-10 and $64.40\pm0.19\%$ and $64.57\pm0.30\%$ on CIFAR-100, at about $0.4$M parameters. At this matched scale the shape model and the simplest value model meet at the top, both above a plain convolution and every per-edge KAN we tested, including the official Gram variant, at roughly a fifth of the parameters. A controlled study attributes the RF-KAN gain to an intrinsically localised oscillatory basis and to content adaptivity, and an ablation that removes the learned shape entirely, leaving only the shared value function, collapses accuracy by over forty points, identifying the learned shape as the load-bearing ingredient at this scale.

item.title: DiffusionBench: On Holistic Evaluation of Diffusion Transformers
item.content:encoded: Diffusion transformer (DiT) research on image generation has converged to a single evaluation setup: class-conditional generation on ImageNet. While methods improve the FID and related metrics, it is increasingly unclear whether they reflect real progress in generative modeling. The natural alternative, i.e., text-to-image (T2I) generation, is perceived as too costly or inconvenient to train and evaluate and is often skipped. We argue that this perception no longer holds. We introduce NanoGen, a unified DiT training and evaluation framework. NanoGen matches state-of-the-art DiT baselines on ImageNet and, with 12 lines of configuration change, also trains competitive text-to-image models. It currently supports RAE, VAE, pixel-space, and MeanFlow diffusion methods under both ImageNet and T2I setups. Under NanoGen, training T2I requires comparable compute to ImageNet. After training 21 latent diffusion models with NanoGen, we observe that method ranking shows no strong correlation between ImageNet and T2I generation: Pearson correlation is between -0.377 and -0.580 across three metrics. This suggests that a method which improves class-conditional ImageNet FID may show no corresponding improvement on T2I, clearly indicating the necessity of evaluating DiTs on both tasks. To this end, we summarize ImageNet and text-to-image results, which yields DiffusionBench, a holistic benchmark for DiT research. We recommend reporting DiffusionBench in place of ImageNet alone: methods that improve DiffusionBench are more likely to reflect broader progress.

item.title: Information-Theoretic Classifier-Free Guidance with Adaptive Schedule Optimization
item.content:encoded: Diffusion models have achieved strong performance in image, text-to-image, and video generation, where conditional generation is often controlled by classifier-free guidance (CFG). CFG improves condition consistency by increasing a guidance weight, but stronger guidance typically reduces diversity and distributional coverage. It remains unclear how this consistency-coverage trade-off should be controlled across the reverse trajectory, since the distribution induced by CFG is not simply the fixed-time tilted distribution given by the guided score field. To address this issue, we propose an information-theoretic framework for CFG schedule optimization. Our approach uses a clean endpoint reference to specify the desired consistency-coverage trade-off, while optimizing the actual distribution induced by the guided sampler toward this reference. We derive trajectory-level formulas to estimate the objective from samples and score evaluations, avoiding explicit density estimation. On ImageNet-512 with EDM-XXL and COCO with SD-XL, the learned schedules achieve competitive or improved trade-offs over constant guidance and allocate guidance selectively across noise levels.

item.title: OmniPath: A Multi-Modal Agentic Framework for Auditing Wheelchair Accessibility
item.content:encoded: For a wheelchair user, a standard blue line on a map is often a broken promise. While platforms like OpenStreetMap (OSM) successfully capture where a path is, they frequently fail to convey how it physically feels to travel on it. This information barrier is problematic for wheelchair users. To solve this issue, we present OmniPath, a system that moves from passive mapping to proactive environmental auditing. Our framework fuses the network topology of OSM with the submeter precision of high-density aerial LiDAR (USGS 3DEP) to create a high-fidelity 3D model of the pedestrian environment. Rather than simply routing a user, our agent virtually traverses the network, analyzing the surface in 0.5 meter increments. It rigorously quantifies physical friction points specifically running slope, cross slope, and vertical discontinuities against ADA compliance standards, calculating a weighted severity score to categorize hazards from ``Mild'' to ``Critical.'' To ensure real world reliability, we validated the system against 200 physical ground truth field surveys across the National Mall using stratified random sampling. The framework demonstrated strong diagnostic reliability for high-severity hazards, achieving F1-scores of 0.60 for Severe and 0.58 for critical categories. By automating this micro-scale inspection, OmniPath identifies the ``invisible'' barriers that standard maps miss, effectively transforming a static dataset into accessibility data source that anticipates accessibility challenges before the user ever leaves home.

item.title: VieSpeaker: A Large-Scale Vietnamese Speaker Recognition Dataset Beyond Visual Dependency
item.content:encoded: Speaker recognition has advanced rapidly with large-scale training datasets, yet Vietnamese remains under-resourced, with existing corpora limited in scale and acoustic diversity. Most large-scale datasets rely on facial cues to link speech with speaker identities, restricting data collection to recordings where speakers appear on camera. We propose a face-independent dataset construction pipeline and introduce VieSpeaker, a large-scale Vietnamese speaker recognition dataset. Our approach leverages textual metadata and large language model reasoning to infer speaker identities from transcripts and contextual information. VieSpeaker contains approximately 902 hours of speech from 4,715 speakers. Experiments show that models trained on VieSpeaker achieve improved robustness and generalization compared to existing Vietnamese datasets. This work demonstrates the feasibility of face-independent dataset construction and provides a new direction for building large-scale speech resources.

item.title: Managing Task Execution for Unknown Workloads in Batteryless IoT: A Hardware-Agnostic Evaluation
item.content:encoded: In recent years, the Internet of Things (IoT) paradigm has been shifting toward batteryless, energy-harvesting architectures. Sustaining reliable operation in these systems requires intelligent management of highly volatile stored energy. As edge applications grow in complexity, traditional energy-aware schedulers struggle with unpredictable workloads due to their reliance on static execution thresholds or pre-measured, hardware-specific task profiles. To overcome this, we propose two novel, hardware-agnostic dynamic scheduling strategies treating applications as a "black box," requiring no prior energy information: a model-free Reinforcement Learning (RL) agent and an on-the-fly Approximated Prediction (AP) method. We evaluate these methods against an adaptive task rate approach (AsTAR) and optimized static thresholds using a custom-built, physically accurate simulation framework driven by real-world solar data and dynamic LoRa transmission profiles. Rather than claiming universal superiority, our analysis exposes the distinct operational trade-offs of each method: the AP approach delivers lightweight, near-oracle task throughput; the RL agent provides tunable survival-execution balancing; and AsTAR excels at execution pacing across long energy gaps. Finally, we demonstrate that while these advanced strategies provide critical resilience for severely constrained systems with small capacitors, devices with larger energy buffers can efficiently rely on simpler, less computationally expensive static policies.

item.title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
item.content:encoded: Vision-language-action (VLA) models can learn manipulation skills from demonstrations, but their capabilities are bounded by the skills in the training data. We present InSight, a framework that unlocks autonomous skill acquisition by rendering VLAs steerable at the primitive-action level (e.g., "move gripper to the bowl", "lift upward", "pour the bottle"). InSight consists of two primary stages: (1) an automated segmentation pipeline that partitions demonstrations into labeled primitives via VLM plan decomposition and end-effector poses to enable VLA primitive steerability, and (2) a VLM-guided data flywheel that identifies missing primitives required to accomplish a novel task, autonomously attempts demonstrations of the missing primitives with VLM-proposed low-level control, and automatically labels, stores, and integrates successful demonstrations into the VLA training set. We evaluate InSight across simulation and real-world manipulation tasks, including block flipping, drawer closing, sweeping, twisting, and pouring, without any human demonstrations of these target skills. Once learned, these primitives can be composed to execute novel, long-horizon tasks without additional human demonstrations. Our findings demonstrate that primitive steerability provides a practical foundation for continual skill acquisition in VLA policies. Project website: https://insight-vla.github.io.

item.title: DramaDirector: Geometry-Guided Short Drama Generation
item.content:encoded: Short dramas, with their rapid shot rhythms, dialogue-driven focus shifts, and demanding cinematographic grounding, pose challenges that prompt-level or text-only video generation pipelines struggle to meet. We study plot-to-short-drama generation, where a global plot and local context are transformed into visually grounded multi-shot videos. We propose DramaDirector, a geometry-grounded framework that lets the planner borrow cinematographic geometry from a gallery of real short-drama shots indexed by depth and pose. DramaDirector decouples each shot into static visual and dynamic narrative conditions, trains the planner with schema-constrained SFT and GRPO under a learned text-visual alignment reward, and retrieves depth-pose references to guide first-frame generation and image-to-video synthesis. We also introduce DramaBoard, a benchmark built from 35 live-action dramas, 2.8K episodes, and 81K shots, with structured storyboards and multi-dimensional evaluation protocols. Experiments show that DramaDirector improves over representative multi-agent and video generation baselines on faithfulness, consistency, and controllability. Our code is released at: https://github.com/iLearn-Lab/DramaDirector

item.title: 深度神经网络的特征学习一致性与统计分析
item.content:encoded:
深度神经网络在特征学习和预测上具有与传统模型相当的一致性。



摘要： 本文从统计学角度探讨了深度神经网络（DNN）在特征学习和预测一致性方面的表现。研究发现，对于输入输出维度和隐藏神经元数量随样本量呈次线性增长的DNN架构，在学习分层组合目标函数时，仍能保持特征学习的一致性。即使在参数数量超过训练样本数量的过参数化情况下，这种一致性依然成立。实验表明，这类DNN在图像分类任务中表现优异，且广泛使用的CNN结构如AlexNet、VGGNet、ResNet等均符合次线性结构。研究还证明了这类DNN在大样本下具备通用逼近能力，解释了大规模深度学习模型在图像数据上成功的原因。

item.title: RaMem：解决长期记忆中上下文丢失问题的框架
item.content:encoded:
RaMem通过上下文重建提升长期记忆的检索有效性。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为RaMem的框架，用于解决LLM代理在长期交互中因记忆上下文丢失而导致的证据无效问题。RaMem通过四个阶段——证据锚定、回忆条件诱导、有效性感知检索和上下文保留合成——确保记忆片段在检索时仍能保持其原始上下文信息，从而提高记忆的有效性和准确性。实验表明，RaMem在多个基准测试中显著优于现有方法，平均F1得分提升超过10%。

item.title: 正则化M估计在再生核希尔伯特空间中的理论研究
item.content:encoded:
研究正则化M估计的理论与收敛速率，适用于多种损失函数。



摘要： 本文提出了再生核希尔伯特空间中正则化M估计的全面理论。在对损失函数提出温和条件的基础上，证明了估计量的存在性与可测性，覆盖了广泛凸性和非凸性损失函数，包括稳健的有界损失。进一步证明了由新复杂度度量控制的精确收敛速率，其中方差独立于模型误设，而偏差依赖于学习文献中已知的源条件参数。针对张量积Sobolev空间，获得了新的收敛速率，连接到具有主导混合光滑性的函数空间，显著扩展了现有结果，并解释了这些估计量如何规避维数灾难。方法结合了泛函分析和经验过程理论，允许目标函数的渐近线性化，避免了闭式解和全局Lipschitz假设，可能具有独立研究价值。估计器使用C++实现，理论通过数值实验得到支持。

item.title: 基于生成模型的交通微仿真系统Enactor
item.content:encoded:
Enactor实现闭环交通微仿真，提升车辆交互准确性与安全性。



摘要： 本文提出Enactor，一种面向信号交叉口的闭环交通微仿真生成模型。该模型以车辆为中心，将行人作为影响因素而非预测对象，通过极坐标编码动态物体和车道线，并利用带有空间和时间注意力机制的Transformer预测车辆下一步运动轨迹。训练过程中采用闭环课程学习，使模型能处理自身预测结果。实验表明，Enactor在两个交叉口几何结构下的4000秒仿真测试中，显著优于现有基线模型，在速度和行程时间分布上KL散度降低超过一个数量级，且大幅减少闯红灯现象。此外，模型在真实世界数据和多时间步预测任务中也表现出色。

ToolGraph结合DPO提升工具使用代理任务表现。

item.title: 基于多项式Dice损失的医学图像分割方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出多项式Dice损失以提升医学图像分割性能。



摘要： 医学图像分割在医疗影像处理和计算机辅助干预中具有基础性作用，但数据不平衡和小病灶检测仍是主要挑战。Dice Loss通过衡量预测区域与真实区域的重叠度来缓解这些问题。本文提出多项式Dice Loss，通过泰勒展开将Dice Loss表示为多项式形式，从而调整高阶成分对损失函数的贡献。实验表明，该方法在多种分割场景中表现优异，提供了一种简单控制损失形状的方式。

item.title: AI科学家：多智能体系统在科学发现中的角色转变
item.content:encoded:
AI正从工具演变为科学发现的主体，推动科研范式变革。



摘要： 文章探讨了代理型人工智能系统在科学发现中的作用，指出其不仅能够辅助科研流程，还可能发展为具备自主生成假设和验证能力的“AI科学家”。这种转变是质变而非量变。文章以Denario框架为例，展示了多智能体系统如何加速科研过程并探索人类难以触及的模型空间。同时讨论了AI对作者身份、同行评审及人类科学家角色的影响，并提出应将AI视为知识主体进行治理，而非仅作为工具。

item.title: 基于参考的扩散模型高效生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Sparse Context提升参考图像生成效率。



摘要： 本文针对基于参考的扩散模型在运行时计算成本高的问题，提出Sparse Context方法，通过保留少量参考令牌构建稀疏表示，提升生成效率。实验表明，在不牺牲视觉质量的前提下，该方法使多参考生成速度提升4倍，单参考生成速度提升2倍。训练阶段引入随机令牌丢弃策略增强模型对部分参考表示的鲁棒性，推理时采用任务感知的令牌选择策略，实现更灵活的控制。

item.title: MambaADv2：基于多尺度DSS模块的多类无监督异常检测框架
item.content:encoded:
MambaADv2通过多尺度DSS模块实现高效异常检测。



摘要： 本文提出MambaADv2，一种面向多类无监督异常检测的框架。该框架结合预训练编码器与Mamba启发的解码器，引入多尺度的Duality-enhanced State Space (DSS)模块，有效建模全局依赖与局部表征。DSS模块融合并行级联的Hybrid State Space (HSS)块和频率增强卷积操作，提升对正常样本的精确重建能力并放大异常偏差。此外，还设计了语义自适应渐进扫描策略，降低特征金字塔中的扫描复杂度，提升检测效率。

item.content:encoded:
研究VAE设计对签名生成潜空间的影响及其性能表现。



摘要： 本文探讨了用于手语生成的变分自编码器（VAE）在架构和训练目标上的设计选择如何影响潜空间结构，以及这些差异如何转化为文本到手语生成的潜在扩散模型的性能。实验基于Phoenix14T数据集，结果显示，生成性能的差异有时比VAE的重建准确性更能被潜空间特性所解释。

item.title: EML Trees Are Universal Approximators
item.content:encoded: The recently introduced EML (Exp-Minus-Log) function acts as continuous analogue of NAND gates, providing a compositional building block capable of representing elementary functions. In this work, we study the expressive power of tree-structured compositions of EML functions. We show that such trees enjoy a universal approximation property for functions in $W^{k, \infty}$ for $k \in \mathbb N$, drawing on classical neural network approximation arguments while exploiting the ability to explicitly construct EML trees that mimic polynomial representations. We further propose a learning algorithm for EML-type trees equipped with fitting parameters, and demonstrate its feasibility in practical optimization problems. Our results establish EML trees as a theoretically grounded framework for function approximation.

item.title: CoorDex：实现高自由度人形机器人动态操作的控制方法
item.content:encoded:
CoorDex使高自由度人形机器人在移动中实现精细操作。



摘要： 本文提出CoorDex，一种将高维身体和灵巧手控制转换为协调潜变量控制的学习管道，实现了人形机器人在移动中的高自由度灵巧操作。通过模拟全身和手部演示训练出优越的运动跟踪教师模型，并将其压缩为感知条件下的潜变量先验，用于后续残差强化学习。该方法通过共享任务上下文和独立的肢体-手部残差头，保持自然全身运动的同时提升手指接触可靠性。实验表明，CoorDex使Unitree G1人形机器人在移动中完成非停止瓶抓、开门和立方体抓取翻转等复杂操作，优于传统方法。

item.title: 基于模型压缩与专家混合的高效神经网络优化方法
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种新型神经网络压缩方法，提升模型效率。



摘要： 近年来，深度神经网络在多个领域广泛应用，但其在嵌入式和边缘设备上的训练与部署面临内存和计算资源受限的挑战。为解决这一问题，本文提出一种两阶段的模型压缩方法：首先通过剪枝和量化等技术显著减小模型规模，随后利用专家混合（Mixture of Experts）机制将压缩后的模型进行路由以提升性能，同时保持推理效率。实验结果表明，该方法在多个基准数据集上有效降低了卷积神经网络的计算量和参数数量，且精度损失极小。

item.title: 大语言模型不确定性量化与分类研究
item.content:encoded:
研究大语言模型的不确定性来源及量化方法。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型（LLMs）在推理和生成任务中固有的随机性带来的可信度挑战，提出了一种细粒度的不确定性分类体系，将不确定性归因于输入、参数、词元和解码过程等不同层级。同时，对现有的不确定性量化（UQ）方法进行了分类，并构建了一个涵盖多种生成场景和评估指标的综合评估框架。通过在多个主流LLM家族上进行实验，验证了不同UQ方法在不同任务和生成设置下的有效性，发现基于共识的方法表现最佳，且模型规模越大，不确定性估计越低，揭示了LLM不确定性的一个经验尺度规律。该研究为理论分析与实际应用之间建立了桥梁，提供了一种系统量化LLM不确定性的工具。

item.title: 基于手部关键点数据的未知身份识别研究
item.content:encoded:
研究使用手部关键点数据进行未知身份识别，验证了紧凑描述符的有效性。



摘要： 本研究在Multi View Leap2 Hand Pose (ML2HP)数据集上，基于Leap Motion Controller 2 (LMC2)的手部关键点数据，探索了在主体级未知身份识别协议下的识别方法。研究保留了原始几何表示，并通过指尖到掌心距离和手掌归一化的指间角度描述符进行增强。评估采用Leave-One-Subject-Out (LOSO)协议，在每次外层循环中排除一个主体作为未知测试样本。为避免对真实未知主体进行调优，通过内部验证步骤选择未知拒绝阈值。比较了树集成基线与两种神经网络模型，结果显示Extra Trees仍是最佳方法，表明该基准的主要挑战在于已知与未知样本的得分分离，而非仅是已知主体的区分。结果支持了基于关键点的紧凑、可解释描述符在小群体数据集上的可行性。

item.title: 基于标注者特征的道德文本分类模型研究
item.content:encoded:
模型通过学习标注者特征提升道德分类准确性。



摘要： 本文探讨了在社交媒体文本中理解道德价值观对道德判断形成的重要性。尽管监督式NLP模型在分类任务上表现优异，但多数方法通过聚合多个标注者的标签来简化问题，忽视了任务本身的主观性。实际中，标注者间的分歧可能源于个人观点或文本本身的模糊性，尤其在短文本如推文中更为明显。为此，研究者在预训练语言模型中引入一个学习标注者特性的层，提升了个体标注的预测效果，并揭示了标注者道德视角的深层信息。研究表明，基于聚合标签的模型可能掩盖了差异，误导性能评估。总体而言，标注分歧反映了任务的主观性，而建模个体视角有助于提升文本道德分类的效果。

item.title: 神经分类树提升模型对少数子组的鲁棒性
item.content:encoded:
神经分类树通过结构编码子组信息，提升模型在少数子组上的表现。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为神经分类树（NCT）的框架，旨在解决机器学习模型在少数子组上表现不佳的问题。该方法通过树状结构编码子组信息，根据样本预测的正确性将其路由到‘易’或‘难’节点，并将这些路径作为伪标签用于下一轮迭代，从而分离冲突的子组，无需显式子组标注。实验表明，NCT不仅提升了模型的鲁棒性，还提供了良好的可解释性，能够清晰地映射模型结构与数据潜在分组之间的关系。

item.title: 多模态融合框架提升机器人手术执行错误检测精度
item.content:encoded:
多模态数据与文本提示结合提升手术错误检测效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种统一的执行错误检测框架，利用视频、运动学数据和描述性文本提示进行多模态融合。通过活动提示整合手势级活动、器械-物体交互及错误定义的描述语言，并引入基于手术活动预训练的视觉嵌入，对比了对比语言-图像嵌入与传统图像嵌入的效果。该框架在JIGSAWS和SAR-RARP50数据集上分别实现了5%和16.6%的F1分数提升，验证了多模态数据与结构化文本提示结合在手术错误检测中的有效性。

item.title: 基于高速公路误差传播的预测编码网络深度训练
item.content:encoded:
HEP提升PCN深度训练效果，实现多层MLP准确分类。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为高速公路误差传播（HEP）的方法，用于解决预测编码网络（PCNs）在深度架构中学习信号衰减的问题。通过引入反馈矩阵，HEP在每一层推理步骤中提供与深度无关的校正信号，从而克服传统PCN中误差随深度指数衰减的限制。实验表明，HEP能够有效训练多达128层的多层感知机（MLP），并在MNIST和Fashion-MNIST数据集上表现出对深度不敏感的高准确性。

item.title: RS-Gen：一种多阶段图像生成代理框架
item.content:encoded:
RS-Gen提升图像生成模型的逻辑推理与知识应用能力。



摘要： 文章介绍了一种名为RS-Gen的多阶段图像生成代理框架，旨在解决现有图像模型在处理模糊意图、逻辑推理和分布外知识时表现不佳的问题。该框架通过引入“提问与求解”的闭环机制，实现对逻辑问题和知识缺口的精准识别，并自主规划行动以弥补信息不足。实验表明，RS-Gen在多个基准测试中显著提升了基础图像生成与编辑模型的性能，使Qwen-Image和Qwen-Image-Edit-2511达到开源模型的最先进水平。

item.title: KaLM-Reranker-V1：高效且灵活的重排序模型
item.content:encoded:
KaLM-Reranker-V1实现高效重排序，提升检索系统性能。



摘要： KaLM-Reranker-V1是一种高效的重排序模型，通过解耦查询与文档计算，提升了部署效率和灵活性。该模型基于编码器-解码器架构，使用编码器预编码文档，解码器建模用户意图，并通过交叉注意力捕捉相关性。其在BEIR、MIRACL和LMEB数据集上表现出色，尤其在小规模模型中也具备竞争力。三种不同参数量的版本展示了其在性能与效率之间的良好平衡。

item.title: 合成语音水印引发的检测漏洞与修复方案
item.content:encoded:
水印导致检测模型误判，影响合成语音真实性判断。



摘要： 本文探讨了合成语音水印在检测系统中可能引发的问题。当合成语音被水印而真实语音未被水印时，检测模型会错误地将水印视为伪造的标志，从而导致三种问题：泛化能力下降、去水印逃避和真实语音被误判为伪造。实验表明，这种缺陷可以通过重新训练模型以消除水印与伪造之间的关联来解决。研究还提供了包含干净语音和水印语音的数据集，用于后续研究。

查看原文

item.title: GroundEval：评估智能体是否基于正确证据进行推理的框架
item.content:encoded:
GroundEval通过追踪智能体的证据路径，检测其推理是否依赖有效信息。



摘要： GroundEval是一个无需人工评判的框架，用于评估智能体在受控证据下的表现。它通过生成问题、记录智能体的推理过程并评分，来检测智能体是否依赖正确的证据。研究发现，即使智能体给出看似合理的答案，也可能未真正获取所需信息，导致评分极低。该框架关注三个关键问题：智能体是否在声明缺失前进行了检查、是否仅基于当时可用证据推理、是否使用了正确的因果机制。GroundEval揭示了传统评估方法无法检测到的盲点，提供结构化诊断以提高评估透明度。

item.title: 交互统计决策中的δ显式最小极大分位数理论
item.content:encoded:
研究交互统计决策中的最小极大分位数理论及其应用。



摘要： 本文提出了一种δ显式的最小极大分位数理论，用于解决交互统计决策中的罕见但关键的失败问题。通过分析最小极大分位数与最小极大风险之间的关系，作者引入了高概率的交互Fano方法和Le Cam方法，并展示了互信息隐私可以在同一框架下处理。研究还针对高斯均值估计和K臂高斯老虎机问题，推导了明确依赖于置信度δ和隐私预算的最小极大分位数下界，揭示了隐私对误差尺度的影响。

item.title: 人工智能在电子废弃物智能回收中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
AI提升电子垃圾回收效率，实现精准分类。



摘要： 本文探讨了人工智能在电子废弃物智能回收中的应用，特别是在快速处理大量电子垃圾方面的作用。通过结合人工智能与循环经济，研究聚焦于智能手机作为案例，采用迁移学习技术对AlexNet模型进行微调，并在小规模数据集上进行实验。通过调整学习率、优化器和数据增强策略，模型在98%的准确率下表现出色，有效降低了电子垃圾分拣错误率，展示了人工智能在智能循环经济中的巨大潜力。

item.title: 实体级成员推理：评估大语言模型中的隐私与版权风险
item.content:encoded:
研究提出实体级成员推理方法，评估大语言模型是否暴露实体信息。



摘要： 本文探讨了大语言模型（LLMs）在隐私泄露和版权合规方面的风险，提出了一种新的实体级成员推理方法。该方法通过询问模型是否接触过特定实体信息，来评估其训练数据的敏感性。研究在仅可观察生成文本的黑盒环境下进行，提出了五种基于有限实体线索的推理策略，并构建了实体级数据集进行实验。结果显示，该方法在人物实体上的AUC可达0.97，相比现有方法在平衡准确率上提升了6.0%至17.5%。

item.title: 基于计算机视觉的腹腔镜摄像导航评估框架研究
item.content:encoded:
建立腹腔镜摄像导航评估框架，结合临床需求与技术可行性。



摘要： 本研究旨在定义并评估腹腔镜摄像导航（LCN）的评估方法，通过构建14个关键方面的分类体系，包括构图、可视性、方向性、动态性和安全性等。研究人员对每个方面进行了技术成熟度评估，并通过问卷调查收集23名外科医生对各方面的临床重要性评分。结果显示，视野、焦点和定位等基础方面被普遍认为最为重要。研究提出了一种“临床重要性与CV技术成熟度”矩阵，为未来自动化评估系统开发提供了优先方向，有助于提升手术培训效率和安全性。
