item.title: Deployment-complete benchmarking
item.content:encoded: Benchmarks increasingly guide deployment, procurement and scientific screening, yet a score supports only the response it records, not necessarily the deployment action. We introduce deployment-complete benchmarking, which tests whether benchmark evidence determines a deployment action. A benchmark is complete for a claim exactly when the action is constant on each evidence fiber; mixed fibers expose missing deployment information, and completion curves quantify the evidence required to resolve ambiguity. In controlled response spaces, benchmark-channel conformal coverage of 94.98% transferred poorly to an unmeasured deployment channel (10.07%), whereas response-rank intervals achieved 94.91% coverage; even zero benchmark error certified only 45.4% of candidates at the largest residual size. Public audits revealed incompleteness, including 97.9% mixed Tox21 fibers and zero median certifiable fraction in main Matbench and JARVIS audits. In held-out replays, certify-then-acquire reduced false decisions from 1.19% to 0.027% in Tox21 and from 20.3% to 0.128% in JARVIS, while changing model choice and identifying deployment-relevant probes. Deployment-ready benchmarks should report evidence, supported actions, ambiguity and completion cost rather than scores alone.

item.title: QUIET: A Multi-Blank Cascaded Story Cloze Benchmark for LLM Creative Generation Capability
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) face a dual challenge in creative capability evaluation: existing benchmarks (e.g., Story Cloze Test, HellaSwag) measure models' discriminative ability over narrative continuation using multiple-choice recognition paradigms, rather than directly measuring creative generation capability; rubric-based scoring and LLM-as-Judge methods rely on subjective dimension assessment or natural language model outputs, and cannot provide objective, automated scoring mechanisms. This paper proposes QUIET (Quality Understanding via Interlocked Evaluation Testing), a diagnostic benchmark for LLM creative capability based on multi-blank cascaded story cloze. QUIET sets N blanks (10-20) in a story with complete structure, with each blank accompanied by an explicit content constraint, and cascade dependency relationships between blanks -- the content filled into earlier blanks constrains the feasible solution space for later blanks. The evaluated model (or human participants) fills all blanks in open-ended generation mode; the results are scored by an information-theoretic automated scoring protocol without human grading. The scoring protocol directly operationalizes the "calibrated surprise" theoretical framework (Zou & Xu, 2026a). For each blank k, a composite score is computed: score = satisfy * (1 + lambda * surprise), where lambda = 1.0. Here, "satisfy" measures how well the blank filling satisfies the content constraint (objective logical reasoning judgment, not subjective aesthetic scoring), and "surprise" measures the degree of surprise given that the constraint is satisfied. Creative answers that do not satisfy the constraint score zero; answers that satisfy the constraint but are mediocre score low; answers that satisfy the constraint and are surprising score high.

item.title: Full-4D: Generating Full-Scope 4D Scenes from a Single-View Video
item.content:encoded: Generating 4D scenes from a single-view video is inherently ill-posed: a single viewpoint lacks the information needed to recover a complete, dynamic scene with full coverage. Existing methods are typically limited to monocular videos, simple 3D effects, or only small viewpoint perturbations around the original viewpoint, falling short of true 4D generation. Meanwhile, the lack of large-scale datasets capturing full-scope 4D scenes with synchronized multi-view videos further hinders progress in this direction. We propose a novel single-view video-to-4D framework that casts full-scope 4D generation as a multi-view video synthesis followed by optimization-based 4D reconstruction from the generated views. To instantiate this formulation end-to-end, we make three key contributions. First, we introduce Real-MV-4D, a large-scale dataset of synchronized multi-view videos captured in diverse real-world environments to provide the 4D supervision. Second, we train a multi-view video diffusion model driven by a novel fused time(T)-view(V) attention mechanism that directly embeds geometric reprojection priors and explicit camera conditioning into its view-time interactions. Unlike basic feature fusion, this direct binding strictly aligns the generation process with physical 3D priors to produce a dense, synchronized T$\times $V video grid. Third, rather than relying on non-interactive and inconsistent 2D video interpolations, we lift the synthesized multi-view videos into an explicit 4D representation (i.e. 4DGS), regularized by a Flow Matching Distillation loss that exploits the multi-view prior to improve novel-view rendering. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms existing approaches in both visual fidelity and geometric consistency, enabling full-scope 4D scene generation from single-view videos.

item.title: Language Models Need Sleep
item.content:encoded: Transformer-based large language models are increasingly used for long-horizon tasks; however, their attention mechanism scales poorly with context length. To handle this, we study a sleep-like consolidation mechanism in which a model periodically converts recent context into persistent fast weights before clearing its key-value cache. During sleep, the model performs $N$ offline recurrent passes over the accumulated context and updates the fast weights in its state-space model (SSM) blocks through a learned local rule. During inference, this shifts extra computation to sleep while preserving the latency of wake-time prediction. We test our method on controlled synthetic tasks, including cellular automata and multi-hop graph retrieval, as well as a realistic math reasoning task, on which a regular transformer as well as SSM-attention hybrid models fail. We then show that increasing sleep duration $N$ for our models improves performance, with the largest gains on examples that require deeper reasoning.

item.title: CMAP: Cross-Modal Adaptive Prompting for Multi-Domain Task-Incremental Learning
item.content:encoded: Multi-domain task-incremental learning requires a model to sequentially acquire knowledge across visually diverse domains without forgetting prior tasks, and without access to task identity at inference. Parameter-efficient methods built on frozen vision-language models have made strong progress, yet all existing approaches rely exclusively on visual features for task routing, confidence estimation, and encoder adaptation, leaving CLIP's cross-modal text embedding space entirely unexploited. We address this gap through three contributions. Text-space task routing replaces visual Gaussian matching with cosine similarity to frozen CLIP text prototypes, giving order-independent routing robust to data scarcity at zero parameter cost. Multi-prototype visual-textual confidence replaces single-Gaussian class modeling with K-means visual prototypes and cross-modal alignment scores under task-calibrated thresholds. Symmetric cross-modal gating extends per-layer Gumbel gates to the text encoder conditioned on batch image features, preserving cross-modal alignment on out-of-distribution inputs. On the MTIL benchmark spanning 11 datasets and 1201 classes, our method achieves 74.2% Transfer, 80.5% Average, and 88.7% Last under Order-I, surpassing the prior state of the art by 5.0, 3.7, and 3.0 percentage points with only 2.5M trainable parameters and no external data.

item.title: DeGRe: Dense-supervised Generative Reranking for Recommendation
item.content:encoded: In multi-stage recommender systems, reranking optimizes overall utility by capturing intra-list contextual dependencies, yet its central challenge lies in exploring optimal sequences within an exponentially large permutation space. Recent studies have shifted towards end-to-end generative frameworks, which typically leverage list-wise rewards or preference alignment to guide generator training. However, these methods still face two critical issues. First is the heuristic label bias. Existing methods often construct training targets based on simple rules, such as promoting clicked items to the top, while ignoring causal dependencies within the list context. Second is the credit assignment problem. Sparse list-level posterior rewards fail to directly guide intermediate steps in sequence generation, leading to ambiguous optimization directions. To address these issues, we propose DeGRe (Dense-supervised Generative Reranking), a generative reranking framework that bridges the gap between offline exploration and online efficiency through dense supervision. The core of DeGRe lies in its offline-online decoupled design. During the offline phase, we introduce a Lookahead Evaluator based on cumulative regression, which leverages beam search to actively mine high-value lookahead sequences in the unexposed space. During training, we transform the step-wise value estimations from the evaluator into dense supervision signals and distill them into a lightweight Online Generator. This mechanism enables the generator to internalize lookahead planning capabilities, requiring only a single efficient greedy decoding pass during online inference to approximate the global optimum. Experiments demonstrate that DeGRe outperforms baseline models on public benchmarks and industrial datasets. We have successfully deployed DeGRe on Taobao Flash Shopping, significantly improving online recommendations.

item.title: Pixel-Level Pavement Distress Assessment Using Instance Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Automated pavement distress assessment requires more than image-level classification or coarse bounding box detection, demanding precise localization of thin, branching, and irregular cracks to achieve the geometric precision necessary for maintenance-relevant quantification. This paper presents a vision-based pavement distress analysis system based on Mask R-CNN instance segmentation and evaluates it on UWGB-StreetCrack, a custom field-collected roadway image dataset acquired with a vehicle-mounted smartphone and manually annotated with polygon labels for longitudinal cracks, transverse cracks, alligator cracks, and potholes. Five Detectron2-based Mask R-CNN backbone variants were considered under a consistent fine-tuning protocol. The best-performing model, Mask R-CNN with a ResNet-101 FPN backbone, achieved 84.23% precision, 90.04% recall, and an F1 score of 87.04% under the project-specific bounding-box matching protocol. The same model produced an aggregate predicted crack-area fraction of 2.164%, closely matching the 2.170% ground-truth crack-area fraction. To contextualize the segmentation system against a detector-oriented alternative, a CSPDarknet53-based YOLO detector was also adapted and retrained on the dataset, reaching 27.5% precision and 20.7% recall on the validation protocol. The results show that instance segmentation is a practical direction for field pavement imagery and aggregate crack-area estimation, while also exposing open challenges in annotation consistency, class imbalance, confounder rejection, and mask-level benchmarking.

item.title: From Simulation to Enaction: Post-trained language models recognize and react to their own generations
item.content:encoded: Language models are pretrained as passive predictors with no incentive to model the consequences of their own outputs. Post-training changes this: a model producing its own responses can benefit from recognizing that it is on-policy. We present evidence that post-trained models recognize their on-policy generations, and this recognition is implicitly encoded in their output distributions. In particular, on-policy output distribution entropy is 3--4$\times$ lower than off-policy entropy, across model families and size classes. We trace part of this effect to an internal representation of input surprise, tracking the unlikeliness of the most recent input token according to the model's prior predictions, that causally modulates output entropy. One example of these phenomena can be observed in response to open-ended prompts; post-trained models (unlike pretrained models) collapse their uncertainty over the topic of their upcoming response before the first output token; violating this cached intention with a different-topic prefill results in higher output entropy. We also tested whether models can distinguish on-policy contexts from prefills via explicit verbal report. We find that they can, but that interestingly, this explicit recognition routes through a different mechanism than implicit recognition.

item.title: Merge-Bench: Resolve Merge Conflicts with Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: This paper applies machine learning to the difficult and important task of version control merging. (1) We constructed a dataset, Merge-Bench, of 7938 real-world merge conflict hunks from 1439 GitHub repositories. The ground truth is the merge resolution that developers committed to the repository. Our dataset construction methodology is scalable to arbitrary amounts of data since no manual labeling is required. (2) We trained a model, LLMergeJ, to resolve merge conflicts in Java programs. Our approach uses Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), an online reinforcement learning method, to train a Large Language Model (LLM). (3) We performed two evaluations of the performance of LLMs on resolving merge conflicts. On Java programs, LLMergeJ with 14B parameters outperforms 3 commercial LLMs, trailing only Gemini 2.5 Pro. Across 11 programming languages, commercial LLM performance is largely stable from language to language. The best models correctly resolve less than 60% of merge conflicts.

item.title: SomaliBench Eval: Measuring English-to-Somali Refusal Gaps in Open-Weight Language Models
item.content:encoded: Large language model safety evaluation remains heavily English-centered, leaving low-resource languages under-measured even when models are deployed globally. We evaluate four open-weight instruction-tuned models on SomaliBench v0, a native-author-verified benchmark of 100 harmful-intent prompts paired across English and Somali. Each of Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, Gemma-2-9B-Instruct, Qwen-2.5-7B-Instruct, and Aya-23-8B is run locally with temperature 0 and the same English "helpful, harmless, and honest" (HHH) system prompt. A pinned Claude Sonnet snapshot (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) classifies each response as refused, complied, or unclear; the native author spot-checks a stratified 80-row sample. We find large English-to-Somali refusal gaps for all four models: Llama-3.1-8B (0.90; 95% bootstrap CI [0.85, 0.96]), Aya-23-8B (0.75 [0.67, 0.83]), Qwen-2.5-7B (0.69 [0.59, 0.78]), and Gemma-2-9B (0.38 [0.27, 0.49]). For three models, the dominant Somali non-refusal mode is not fluent harmful compliance but unclear output: empty, wrong-language, or incoherent generations. The native verification spot-check achieves 100% agreement with the judge (Cohen's kappa = 1.00) on the 80 sampled rows. We report aggregate refusal rates, category gaps, and reliability statistics only; raw model generations are retained locally and are not released.

item.title: SAM3-Assisted Training of Lightweight YOLO Models for Precision Pig Farming
item.content:encoded: Deep learning-based object detection has revolutionized Precision Livestock Farming (PLF), yet a critical barrier remains: high-performance Foundation Models (such as SAM 3) are too computationally intensive for edge deployment, while lightweight models (like YOLO) require prohibitive manual annotation efforts. This work proposes a fully automated knowledge distillation pipeline that leverages the Segment Anything Model 3 (SAM 3) to generate zero-shot pseudo-labels for training efficient YOLOv8 detectors. By treating SAM 3 as an offline auto-annotator, we eliminate the manual labeling bottleneck, producing models capable of real-time inference on resource-constrained hardware. We systematically evaluate this approach on the PigLife dataset, comparing SAM 3-supervised models against human-annotated baselines. Results demonstrate that a SAM 3-trained YOLOv8m achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 79.4% without human intervention, while reducing inference latency by approximately 200$\times$ compared to the teacher model. Furthermore, stratified analysis reveals that in low-occlusion scenarios, the automated pipeline achieves detection rates comparable to human benchmarks ($AP_{50} > 99\%$). These findings indicate that foundation models can serve as effective, zero-annotation-cost supervisors, enabling scalable edge computing solutions for smart agriculture.

item.title: Forgotten Words: Benchmarking NeoBERT for Dementia Detection in Low-Resource Conversational Filipino and English Speech
item.content:encoded: Dementia detection from spontaneous speech offers a scalable approach to cognitive screening, yet NLP systems remain predominantly English-centric. This limitation is especially acute in the Philippines, where Filipino-English code-switching is pervasive and no prior work has addressed NLP-based dementia detection. We present the first systematic evaluation of transformer-based dementia detection in Filipino speech and the first assessment of NeoBERT in a clinical NLP setting. To separate language from domain effects, we construct a parallel bilingual dataset of 4,000 DementiaBank-derived transcripts, with Filipino translations produced manually to preserve discourse-level markers of cognitive decline. We evaluate five model families, TF-IDF + LogReg, BERT, NeoBERT, XLM-R, and RoBERTa-Tagalog, under monolingual, zero-shot cross-lingual, and bilingual fine-tuning settings. We find that in-domain performance does not transfer across languages, with English-trained BERT dropping to Macro-F1 = 0.455 on Filipino, and that architectural modernization alone does not improve robustness. Bilingual fine-tuning, however, eliminates cross-lingual degradation across all transformer models, converging to Macro-F1 = 0.969-0.973. These results suggest that multilingual clinical NLP performance is driven primarily by linguistic coverage during training rather than model scale or architecture.

item.title: PDEInvBench: A Comprehensive Dataset and Design Space Exploration of Neural Networks for PDE Inverse Problems
item.content:encoded: Inverse problems in partial differential equations (PDEs) involve estimating the physical parameters of a system from observed spatiotemporal solution fields.Neural networks are well-suited for PDE parameter estimation due to their capability to model function-to-function space transformations. While existing benchmarks of machine learning methods for PDEs primarily focus on the forward problem, there are no similar comprehensive studies and benchmark datasets on PDE inverse problems, i.e., mapping solution fields to underlying physical parameters. We fill this gap by introducing PDEInvBench, a comprehensive benchmark dataset consisting of numerical simulations for both time-dependent and time-independent PDEs across a wide range of physical behaviors and parameters. Our dataset includes evaluation splits that assess performance in both in-distribution and various out-of-distribution settings. Using our benchmark dataset, we comprehensively explore the design space of neural networks for PDE inverse problems along three key dimensions: (1) optimization procedures, analyzing the role of supervised, self-supervised, and test-time training objectives on performance, (2) problem representations, where we study the value of architectural choices with different inductive biases and various conditioning strategies, and (3) scaling, which we perform with respect to both model and data size. Our experiments reveal several practical insights: 1) neural networks perform best with a two-stage training procedure: initial supervision with PDE parameters followed by test-time fine-tuning using the PDE residual, 2) incorporating PDE derivatives as input features consistently improves accuracy, and 3) increasing the diversity of initial conditions in the training data yields greater performance gains than expanding the range of PDE parameters. We make our dataset and codebase publicly available.

item.title: Predicting Stock Price Direction on Earnings Announcement Days using Multi-modal Deep Learning
item.content:encoded: Predicting stock price movements during Earnings Announcements (EAs) is a significant challenge due to market noise and high-impact price discontinuities. In this study, we evaluate whether pre-announcement news sentiment, firm fundamentals, and recent market dynamics jointly predict the directional price movement of equities on EA days. We construct a multi-modal feature space combining 15 fundamental metrics, 3 price-based technical indicators and sentiment scores derived from financial news articles processed using FinBERT. We compare a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network and a Transformer-based architecture against a logistic regression baseline, and further assess all models with and without sentiment features to quantify their incremental value. Our results indicate that while the LSTM demonstrates higher precision through a conservative safe-bet strategy, the Transformer model exhibits superior sensitivity in identifying volatile movements, achieving a higher macro F1-score, with ablation experiments showing a consistent benefit from incorporating news sentiment.

item.title: Causal methods for LLM development and evaluation
item.content:encoded: Large language model (LLM) development is currently driven by large-scale empirical iteration over data mixtures, reward models, routing strategies, and evaluation pipelines. Here, we argue that many central questions in LLM development and evaluation are inherently causal: What is the effect of adding a data domain during pretraining? How do annotator preferences change when LLMs generate text in a different style? Should a prompt be routed to a larger or smaller model given inference cost constraints? In general, causal methods are well-suited to such settings where interventions change outcomes but, surprisingly, are underrepresented in LLM development. Our contribution is threefold: (1) We explain how causal methods can help develop modern LLM development and evaluation: LLM development relies heavily on logged data, which are often subject to confounding and distribution shifts; evaluation uses learned but potentially biased judges; and deployment environments are non-stationary. These conditions make purely predictive approaches fragile and create opportunities for principled identification and estimation methods from causal inference. (2) We further map opportunities for causal methods in the entire LLM development pipeline, including pretraining, alignment, routing, agentic workflows, and evaluation. (3) We discuss new research opportunities around leveraging causal methods for LLM development and evaluation. Overall, we argue that causal methods are potentially underutilized for the LLM development and evaluation pipeline, despite the fact that such methods can ensure a reliable and scientifically grounded design.

item.title: EXPO-FT: Sample-Efficient Reinforcement Learning Finetuning for Vision-Language-Action Models
item.content:encoded: The ability to efficiently and reliably learn new tasks has been a foundational challenge in robotics. Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated strong generalization across diverse manipulation tasks, yet pretrained policies consistently fall short of the reliability required for real-world deployment. Reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tuning offers a promising path to bridge this gap, but existing approaches either train from scratch without fully leveraging pretrained priors, or fine-tune VLAs without achieving the sample efficiency and success rates that practical deployment demands. We present EXPO-FT, a system for stable, sample-efficient RL finetuning of pretrained VLA policies that closes this gap. Our system solves a suite of challenging manipulation tasks, including routing string lights and inserting the plug to light it up, striking a pool ball into a pocket, and inserting a flower into a wine bottle, each requiring combinations of high precision, dynamic actions, and robustness to varied initial states. Our system achieves perfect task performance (30/30 successes) across all evaluated tasks within an average of 19.1 minutes of online robot data, outperforming both prior RL-from-scratch and VLA finetuning approaches. We release an open-source codebase with the aim of facilitating broader adoption of RL finetuning of VLA models in robotics.

item.title: Look Both Ways Before You Cross: Lifting Cross Fields From 2D Visual Priors
item.content:encoded: We present CrossLift, a technique for computing cross fields on meshes guided by visual features in images. We leverage powerful text-to-image priors that are capable of synthesizing images of feature-aligned quad meshes in 2D. We extract this signal as explicit per-pixel directions in the 2D images, which we then back-project to the mesh surface. We aggregate these candidate surface directions by performing two smooth interpolations on the mesh surface (first within each view and second across multiple views). We propose custom confidence-based weights for the candidate directions in each interpolation that allow us to resolve conflicts between candidates on the same face and smoothly interpolate our field to occluded faces. Our method is modular and can be used with many different 2D visual priors. We show additional applications to texture-aligned quad meshing as well as interactive cross-field design using coarse, user-drawn lines as signal. We demonstrate the effectiveness of CrossLift on a diverse set of both organic and mechanical shapes and produce quad meshes that exhibit superior semantic alignment as compared to existing methods. Project page at: https://crosslift.github.io/

item.title: Capture-Calibrate-Coach: A Graph-Based Framework for Knowledge Monitoring Estimation and Adaptive Feedback
item.content:encoded: Effective learning support requires understanding not only what learners know but also how accurately they perceive their own understanding. This metacognitive dimension, known as knowledge monitoring, fundamentally influences self-regulated learning, yet this dimension remains underexplored in current systems. This paper introduces the Capture-Calibrate-Coach (3C) framework for adaptive learning support. The Capture phase extracts learners' perceived knowledge states from open-ended self-reports to construct a heterogeneous graph linking learners and knowledge concepts. The Calibrate phase applies a heterogeneous graph neural network to infer latent perceived states for concepts not explicitly mentioned, enabling systematic knowledge monitoring assessment. The Coach phase classifies learners into five metacognitive patterns and delivers personalized feedback addressing both knowledge gaps and calibration errors. Evaluation with 684 students demonstrates 85.21% AUC in predicting latent perceived states, significantly outperforming baseline methods. A user study with 47 participants shows positive reception of feedback quality, with participants particularly valuing concrete feedback on knowledge gaps and actionable study guidance. These findings advance AI-based learning support toward metacognitive teammates that foster accurate self-awareness while supporting knowledge growth.

item.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
item.content:encoded: Subject-driven image generation aims to synthesize new images that preserve the identity of the given subject while following textual instructions. Existing approaches often encode text and reference images separately. This limits cross-modal reasoning abilities and causes copy-paste artifacts. Recent frameworks that connect multimodal models and diffusion models improve instruction following, but largely overlook identity preservation. To address these limitations, we condition diffusion models on Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) that jointly encode text and reference images, and augment it with VAE-based identity conditioning. A novel Dual Layer Aggregation (DLA) module is designed to aggregate multi-level MLLM features for optimal conditioning, and a multi-stage denoising strategy is applied to progressively balance the semantic information from MLLM and fine-detail identity from VAE during inference. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach harmonizes multimodal understanding with identity preservation, mitigates copy-paste issues, and achieves superior performance regarding human preference on subject-driven image generation. Our project website is available at https://zsh2000.github.io/squeeze-mllm-subject-gen/.

item.title: JacQuant: STE-Free Quantization-Aware Training via Learned Jacobian Surrogates
item.content:encoded: Quantization-aware training (QAT) is widely deployed but typically relies on the Straight-Through Estimator (STE), which passes gradients through non-differentiable quantizers by fiat. This often makes training brittle near bin boundaries and weakly aligned with the actual behavior of the low-precision model. We introduce JacQuant, a QAT framework that learns a lightweight surrogate of the model's local sensitivity to parameter changes and uses it to stabilize and accelerate training within standard variance-reduced optimizers. The surrogate is inexpensive (diagonal or block-diagonal), data-driven, and compatible with common weight and activation quantizers. On code-preserving training phases, we prove convergence for non-convex objectives and obtain linear rates under a PL condition, and we relate the learned sensitivity to end-to-end output fidelity via a simple calibration argument. Across LLM benchmarks at $\leq 2$ bits, JacQuant consistently reaches higher accuracy than STE-based QAT, and the runtime analyses on various models show that the added cost remains negligible under practical group sizes. The method is drop-in and requires no changes to the forward quantizers; our empirical claims are scoped to ultra-low-bit LLM QAT.

item.title: 基于多专家框架的文本属性图欺诈检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CAMERA框架提升文本属性图中的欺诈检测效果。



摘要： 本文针对在线社交和电商平台上欺诈者通过模仿正常用户文本行为进行语义伪装的问题，提出一种无监督的文本属性图欺诈检测方法CAMERA。该方法采用去中心化的多专家架构，每个专家专注于建模不同类型的欺诈线索，并引入上下文感知的门控模型，以自适应地整合不同专家的特征。此外，CAMERA利用欺诈者稀有性的特点，通过专家级目标鼓励学习主流良性模式，从而实现对伪装欺诈者的有效检测。实验表明，CAMERA在四个数据集上均优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于视觉干预的医学多模态大模型幻觉检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出VIHD方法提升医学MLLM幻觉检测效果。



摘要： 本文针对医学多模态大语言模型（MLLM）在诊断中产生的幻觉问题，提出一种基于视觉干预的幻觉检测方法VIHD。该方法通过可视化依赖探测定位关键解码层，利用视觉标记掩码进行语义熵校准，从而更有效地识别幻觉响应。实验表明，VIHD在三个医学VQA基准上优于现有方法，验证了细粒度视觉依赖对幻觉检测的重要性。

item.title: 强化学习在格斗游戏中的动态决策机制研究
item.content:encoded:
研究动态决策框架提升RL在格斗游戏中的表现。



摘要： 本文探讨了在快节奏的格斗游戏如《街头霸王II》中，如何通过让RL代理同时学习动作和执行时长来提升其反应能力。传统方法要求代理以固定间隔做出决策，限制了其适应性。新方法允许代理根据情境动态调整响应时间，实验表明该方法在性能上可媲美固定帧跳策略，并鼓励重复动作模式，但需结合高帧跳值以获得最佳效果。

item.title: 多语言稀疏自编码器的机制化语言控制方法
item.content:encoded:
多语言SAE提升语言控制可靠性，优化层选择策略。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于多语言数据训练的稀疏自编码器（SAEs），以实现更可靠和质量保持的语言控制。研究发现，使用多语言数据训练SAEs能增强跨语言表示，并通过基于多语言对齐和语言可分性的先验层选择规则，提高干预深度的预测准确性。实验在LLaMA-3.1-8B和Gemma-2-9B模型上进行，涵盖机器翻译和跨语言摘要任务，结果表明该方法在语言识别准确性和生成质量之间实现了更好的平衡。

item.title: 高效微调框架ChunkFT：内存优化的子网络优化方法
item.content:encoded:
ChunkFT实现高效内存微调，提升模型优化性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为ChunkFT的内存高效微调框架，通过动态激活工作集重新定义全参数微调。该框架无需修改网络结构即可计算任意子张量的梯度，为优化任意子网络提供了算法基础。理论分析表明其在确定性环境下具有收敛性。实验中，ChunkFT在单块RTX 4090-24GB GPU和双H800-80GB GPU上分别对Llama 3-8B和Llama 3-70B进行微调，仅需13.72GB显存即可完成7B模型的全参数微调。结果表明，ChunkFT在内存使用、运行时间和优化质量方面均优于现有方法，并在语言理解、数学推理和MT-Bench等任务中表现优异，部分情况下甚至超过全参数微调。

item.title: 基于语料库的LLM代理故障诊断系统研究
item.content:encoded:
提出IG系统实现大规模执行轨迹诊断，提升系统性能。



摘要： 文章探讨了LLM代理故障诊断的自动化问题，指出传统方法依赖人工检查，难以发现大规模模式。作者提出Insights Generator（IG）系统，通过多智能体协作分析语料库中的执行轨迹，生成基于证据的自然语言洞察报告。评估显示，使用IG报告的人类专家在支架性能上提升了30.4个百分点，代码代理也表现出稳定提升。IG的架构在检测覆盖度上与现有方法相当，且被专家评为具有更高深度和证据质量。

item.title: 基于变分推断的深度预测编码网络优化
item.content:encoded:
提出一种改进的预测编码网络，提升训练效率和性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种改进的预测编码（PC）网络，通过将其表示为深度分层高斯滤波器（HGFs），恢复了精度加权的信息传递机制，从而实现动态不确定性估计和Hebbian兼容的更新规则。该方法在单一自由能目标下同时学习激活值、权重和精度，无需全局误差信号或迭代计算。实验表明，该方法在FashionMNIST数据集上接近反向传播的训练效率，并在在线学习、数据效率和概念漂移任务中表现更优，为深度预测编码网络提供了可行的变分推断基础。

item.title: 基于物理引导的红外小目标检测点监督方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种物理引导的点监督框架，提升红外小目标检测性能。



摘要： 本文针对红外小目标检测中密集标注成本高、伪标签不稳定和样本分布不平衡的问题，提出一种更自适应和稳定的框架。通过利用热辐射模式与热扩散的内在一致性，引入物理引导的标注策略，将单点标签扩展为可靠的伪掩码。同时，设计双层双更新框架，联合优化检测器权重、样本权重和扩散参数。元分类器动态预测样本损失权重，可微扩散模块通过检测反馈优化伪标签，实现训练与超参数优化的自适应交互。实验表明该方法在多个数据集上实现五倍标注加速，检测精度更高，且仅需30%训练数据即可达到相近性能。

item.title: 双向知识蒸馏在随机森林与深度神经网络间的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
提出双向知识蒸馏方法提升模型性能与可解释性。



摘要： 本文首次系统研究了随机森林（RF）与深度神经网络（DNN）之间的双向知识蒸馏方法，旨在解决大数据环境下模型压缩与可解释性的挑战。通过引入渐进式多阶段蒸馏、多教师集成蒸馏以及不确定性感知的跨范式转移机制，实验表明该方法在分类和回归任务中均取得优异表现。在144组实验中，NN-COMPACT达到98.13%的分类准确率，NN-WIDE在回归任务中获得92.6%的R²分数。该框架提升了模型部署的灵活性，为资源受限环境下的可解释AI提供了新方向。

item.title: 基于历史资产的跨领域视觉语言导航适应框架
item.content:encoded:
提出IDEA框架提升视觉语言导航在非平稳环境中的适应能力。



摘要： 本文针对视觉语言导航（VLN）在非平稳环境下的适应问题，提出了一种新的测试时适应（TTA）框架IDEA。该框架通过积累和组合历史资产，将适应过程转化为知识的持续积累与整合。IDEA引入经过Fisher引导加权优化的软提示，结合领域坐标构建动态资产库，并通过将目标领域投影到历史知识的凸包上，建立跨领域桥梁。实验表明，IDEA在多个基准数据集上均优于现有方法，展示了其无需训练即可通过资产共享实现高效适应的能力。

item.title: ProxySHAP：提升机器学习模型交互估计的高效方法
item.content:encoded:
ProxySHAP在保持高精度的同时提高交互估计效率。



摘要： 本文提出 ProxySHAP，一种用于估计 Shapley 和 Banzhaf 交互的新方法。该方法结合树模型的高样本效率与残差校正策略，理论上实现了多项式时间的干预 TreeSHAP 扩展，有效解决了传统方法中指数级树深度依赖的问题。同时，作者分析了残差调整策略的适用条件，证明其可在不使方差随交互规模指数增长的情况下校正代理偏差。实验表明，ProxySHAP 在大规模数据集上表现优异，优于现有最佳方法 ProxySPEX 和 KernelSHAP-IQ，在小预算和大预算场景中均取得最低误差，并提升了下游可解释性任务的性能。

item.title: 基于门控CNN的轻量级可穿戴跌倒检测系统
item.content:encoded:
提出Gated-CNN模型提升可穿戴设备跌倒检测精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Gated-CNN的轻量级双流架构，用于可穿戴设备的跌倒检测。该模型通过独立处理加速度计和陀螺仪数据，结合sigmoid门控模块、全局平均池化层和共享分类头，有效提升了跌倒特征的识别能力。在多个数据集上的实验表明，该模型在离线和实时测试中均优于Transformer基线模型，取得了高达97%的F1分数和98%的准确率，证明了其在智能手表上的高效性和实用性。

item.title: 语言模型中的答案复制现象及其对推理性能的影响
item.content:encoded:
语言模型通过复制最后数字实现高准确率，而非依赖逻辑推理。



摘要： 研究发现，在小型语言模型中，即使打乱推理步骤，模型仍能保持较高准确率。关键在于模型会复制答案前的最后一个数字，而非真正进行逻辑推理。实验显示，正确答案的存在对准确率有显著影响，且即使中间推理正确，若最终数字错误，准确率也会大幅下降。这种复制机制在算术任务中尤为明显，但在非算术任务中效果减弱。研究揭示了链式思维提示的局限性，并指出其可能带来的评估偏差。

item.title: 评估AI代理在企业环境中的安全漏洞
item.content:encoded:
研究测试AI代理在企业环境中对渐进式攻击的敏感性。



摘要： 本文介绍了Boiling the Frog基准，用于评估部署在企业及办公环境中的工具型AI模型是否容易受到渐进式攻击。每个场景从无害的工作空间编辑开始，随后引入具有风险的请求。该基准关注多轮状态化评估，通过控制对话序列中的风险位置来判断生成结果是否变得不安全。研究结果显示，在九个模型中，攻击成功率平均为44.4%，部分模型如Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite的攻击成功率高达92.9%。研究还基于欧盟AI法案和通用AI行为准则进行了分类评估。

item.title: 基于LLM的优化系统在多领域任务中的表现
item.content:encoded:
单一LLM优化系统在多个领域任务中表现优异。



摘要： 本文展示了一个基于大型语言模型的优化系统，能够在不同领域任务中取得卓越效果。该系统通过将优化问题转化为文本优化任务，并利用评分函数进行评估，实现了单任务搜索、多任务搜索以及跨任务迁移学习。实验结果显示，该系统在六个不同任务中均达到最佳性能，包括提升AI模型准确性、降低云成本、生成高效CUDA内核等。研究还发现，提供额外信息能显著提高优化效率，且多任务搜索优于独立优化。该系统为通用问题解决提供了新范式，已开源供研究使用。

item.title: 动态威胁检测代理（DTDA）提升网络安全防御能力
item.content:encoded:
DTDA通过自适应机制提升威胁检测精度与效率。



摘要： 文章介绍了动态威胁检测代理（DTDA），这是一种持续运行的自适应安全分析工具，能够跨微软Defender平台调查安全事件，发现隐藏威胁并生成可解释的检测逻辑。DTDA结合统一活动时间线、版本化LLM提示合约、规划执行调查循环以及动态警报生成机制，在实际部署中展现出高精度和高效性。在120天的在线评估中，DTDA达到80.1%的准确率，并为约15%的调查事件生成新警报。离线评估中，DTDA使用GPT-5.4恢复恶意活动的F1值为0.78，优于GPT-4.1和基线模型。操作上，DTDA能在28分钟内完成单次调查，成本仅为2.04美元，失败率仅0.38%。结果表明，自主代理可在生产环境中有效识别被遗漏的恶意行为。

item.title: REPA-P：一种无需教师模型的物理一致性扩散框架
item.content:encoded:
REPA-P通过中间特征对齐提升物理约束，显著提高PDE求解性能。



摘要： 本文提出REPA-P，一种无需教师模型的物理一致性扩散框架，通过在训练过程中对中间层特征进行物理残差约束，提升模型对偏微分方程（PDE）的求解能力。该方法在多个PDE任务中表现出色，如Darcy流、拓扑优化、静电势和湍流通道流等，有效加速收敛、降低物理残差并增强分布外鲁棒性。REPA-P采用轻量级投影头，在不增加推理开销的情况下实现物理一致性。实验表明，仅监督少量中间层即可获得主要收益，并与输出层物理损失互补。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于得分的单步均流策略优化方法
item.content:encoded:
SOM在在线强化学习中实现高效策略优化。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于得分的单步均流策略优化方法（SOM），用于解决在线强化学习中的计算效率问题。传统扩散和流匹配方法依赖多步去噪，导致推理时间过长。而SOM通过从Q函数中直接构造目标速度场，避免了对目标分布样本的依赖，从而提高了效率。实验表明，SOM在运动任务中表现优异，且训练和推理时间显著减少。

item.title: 提升视觉基础模型在注视跟随任务中的 gaze reasoning 能力
item.content:encoded:
提出新训练机制增强 VFMs 的 gaze reasoning 能力。



摘要： 本文针对视觉基础模型（VFMs）在注视跟随任务中虽能有效理解场景，但对 gaze reasoning 能力提升有限的问题，提出一种新的训练机制。该方法包括基于头部条件的局部 LoRA 和非锥形惩罚，以增强头部分支对 gaze cues 的学习能力。实验表明，该方法在 GazeFollow 和 VAT 数据集上表现优异，尤其在目标不显著时效果更明显。研究为未来注视跟随任务提供了重要参考。

item.title: 基于频域正则化的多模态大语言模型对抗攻击方法
item.content:encoded:
提出FRA-Attack提升多模态模型对抗样本的跨模型迁移能力。



摘要： 本文针对多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在基于迁移的定向攻击中的脆弱性，提出一种新的对抗攻击方法FRA-Attack。该方法通过频域正则化策略解决现有方法在空间域特征冗余和依赖特定代理模型梯度的问题。FRA-Attack采用高通DCT目标抑制冗余全局结构，集中损失于承载内在视觉焦点的高频段；同时引入频率域梯度正则化（FGR），以几何频率坐标调控梯度，实现模型无关的低通正则化。实验表明，FRA-Attack在15个主流MLLMs上表现出色，尤其在GPT-5.4、Claude-Opus-4.6和Gemini-3-flash中达到领先性能。

item.title: 动态压缩框架Meta-Soft解决KV缓存效率与信息丢失问题
item.content:encoded:
Meta-Soft提升KV缓存效率并减少信息丢失。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Meta-Soft的动态压缩框架，用于解决大语言模型中KV缓存线性增长导致的内存爆炸和解码效率下降问题。现有方法依赖静态参数评估KV对的重要性，无法适应不同输入，且丢弃的KV对造成不可逆的信息损失。Meta-Soft通过构建可学习的正交基矩阵和使用Gumbel-Softmax选择器网络，动态合成最相关的软令牌，并引入基于注意力流的整合机制，将被移除标记的语义信息重新分配到保留标记中，从而有效保持上下文连贯性。实验表明，该方法优于现有先进方法，为KV缓存压缩提供了新思路。

item.title: MXFP4量化误差分解及其对强化学习训练的影响
item.content:encoded:
MXFP4量化误差可分解为三部分，影响RL训练不同路径。



摘要： 本文研究了MXFP4量化误差对大型语言模型强化学习后训练的影响。通过理论分析和实证研究，将量化误差精确分解为三个组成部分：由2的幂次舍入引起的“尺度偏移”、由零值截断引起的“死区截断”以及由四比特网格舍入引起的“网格噪声”。这些误差分别影响强化学习的不同训练路径：尺度偏移在反向传播中累积，影响梯度精度；死区截断降低回放质量；网格噪声增加策略熵。文章提出针对性的修正方法，如宏块缩放、异常值回退和自适应量化噪声，有效恢复BF16精度并提升性能。

item.title: EnvFactory：通过自动化环境构建与轨迹合成提升代理强化学习效果
item.content:encoded:
EnvFactory解决LLM工具使用训练中的两大难题，提升模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出EnvFactory，一个全自动框架，用于解决代理强化学习（Agentic RL）中环境构建和训练数据稀缺的问题。EnvFactory能够从真实资源中自主探索并验证状态化、可执行的工具环境，并通过拓扑感知采样和校准精炼生成自然多轮轨迹，从而生成具有隐含意图的查询。在仅使用85个验证环境的情况下，EnvFactory生成了2,575条SFT和RL轨迹，相比以往方法使用的环境数量少5倍，但仍显著提升了Qwen3系列模型的性能，在多个基准测试中取得最高15%的提升。EnvFactory为代理强化学习提供了可扩展、可扩展且稳健的基础。

item.title: FastKernels：解决LLM推理框架中基准测试与实际应用的不匹配问题
item.content:encoded:
FastKernels优化LLM推理性能，提升生产环境效率。



摘要： 本文提出FastKernels，一个针对LLM推理的最小化、生产级内核基准测试工具。它覆盖46种代表性架构，涵盖96.2%的HuggingFace Transformers模型。FastKernels不仅作为基准测试平台，还具备与vLLM和SGLang相当的性能，并在部分架构上表现更优。研究发现，当前最先进的LLM代理仅能带来0.94倍的性能提升，表明现有基准测试与实际应用存在显著偏差。FastKernels旨在推动生成式代理的性能提升直接转化为生产系统的吞吐量改进。

item.title: 基于因果预测的帕金森病药物管理框架CASCADE
item.content:encoded:
CASCADE框架提升PD药物剂量预测可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出CASCADE框架，通过结合分类任务中的不确定性来动态调整回归任务的预测区间，以提高帕金森病患者药物剂量预测的可靠性。该方法利用Venn-Abers多概率不确定性映射到非一致性分数，实现连续风险适应。实验表明，该框架在高信心患者中预测区间比标准方法窄38.9%，同时确保低信心情况下的覆盖度，有效弥合了临床决策与剂量预测之间的差距。

item.title: 药物敏感性预测的瓶颈源于评估指标而非药物表征
item.content:encoded:
药物敏感性预测停滞是因评估指标问题，非药物表征不足。



摘要： 本文指出，精准肿瘤学中药物敏感性预测的停滞并非由于药物表征不够复杂，而是因为标准评估指标（全局皮尔逊相关系数）受药物间效力差异影响，导致模型无法有效学习细胞特异性信息。通过分析不同数据集发现，使用药物仅特征即可达到与复杂药物表征相当的效果。进一步实验表明，将作用机制作为训练约束而非特征输入，能显著提升靶向激酶抑制剂的预测性能。研究提出两种可部署策略：机制分层训练和响应匹配，以恢复药物盲预测中的主要预测优势。

item.title: 大型语言模型的OOD对齐失败检测与改进方法
item.content:encoded:
研究提出MOOD基准，提升LLM监控中OOD对齐失败的检测能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在分布外（OOD）情境下的对齐失败问题，并引入了一个名为MOOD的基准来评估监控系统的有效性。由于现有模型在训练数据之外的失败难以发现，研究者通过限制训练集并构建多个测试集进行实验。结果表明，现有的安全分类器在OOD场景下表现不佳，因此提出将安全分类器与OOD检测器结合的方法。实验显示，结合马氏距离和困惑度的检测器可提升召回率。此外，研究还发现，结合OOD检测的监控系统在不同模型规模下均表现出良好的扩展性，优于单纯增加参数量的方案。该工作强调了OOD检测在LLM监控中的重要性。

item.title: 基于流匹配的三角形网格信号生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需依赖网格划分的信号生成方法，提升模型泛化能力。



摘要： 本文研究了一种无需依赖特定三角剖分的信号生成方法，通过将流匹配（FM）框架应用于三角形网格，实现对不同网格结构的有效适应。作者提出了一个与三角剖分无关的噪声分布，并基于马特恩过程构建了高效的采样算法。同时，采用PoissonNet作为去噪器，提升了模型在复杂任务中的表现。实验表明，该方法能够生成高质量且多样化的网格信号，适用于超过一百万三角形的复杂模型。

item.title: 基于KAN+XGBoost的电力价格预测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出KAN+XGBoost混合模型提升电力价格预测精度。



摘要： 本文针对电力市场价格波动大、非线性特征强的问题，提出一种结合Kolmogorov-Arnold网络（KAN）与XGBoost的混合预测框架。该模型利用KAN的全局非线性建模能力和XGBoost的局部鲁棒性，有效捕捉长期依赖和短期波动。在澳大利亚国家电力市场的真实数据上进行实验，结果表明该模型相比SARIMAX、LSTM、单独KAN和XGBoost等基准方法，在MAE指标上分别降低约12%和50%，验证了其在动态电力市场中的有效性与稳定性。

item.title: 小数据重复训练的计算优势研究
item.content:encoded:
小数据重复训练可节省计算资源，提升优化效率。



摘要： 本文研究了“小数据与大数据的差距”现象，即在较少样本上重复训练相比使用更大数据集可以节省计算资源。该现象在多种算法任务、架构和优化器中均被观察到，且无法用现有理论解释。研究认为，这种加速源于采样偏差带来的分层增长策略，尤其在数据量较小时更为明显。通过理论分析和实验验证，研究指出小数据重复不仅是在数据不足时的权宜之计，更可作为优化中的有利归纳偏置，尤其适用于推理任务。

item.title: CRAFT: Critic-Refined Adaptive Key-Frame Targeting for Multimodal Video Question Answering
item.content:encoded: Grounded multi-video question answering over real-world news events requires systems to surface query-relevant evidence across heterogeneous video archives while attributing every claim to its supporting source. We introduce CRAFT (Critic-Refined Adaptive Key-Frame Targeting), a query-conditioned pipeline that combines dynamic keyframe selection, per-video ASR with multilingual fallback, and a hybrid critic loop to iteratively verify and repair claims before consolidation. The pipeline integrates UNLI temporal entailment, DeBERTa-v3 cross-claim screening, and a Llama-3.2-3B adjudicator, with a final citation-merging stage that emits each fact once with all supporting source identifiers. On MAGMaR 2026, CRAFT achieves the best overall average (0.739), reference recall (0.810), and citation F1 (0.635). We further evaluate on a MAGMaR-style conversion of WikiVideo with 52 non-overlapping event queries, where CRAFT also performs strongly (0.823 Avg), showing that its claim-centric evidence aggregation generalizes beyond MAGMaR. Ablations show that atomic claims, ASR, and the critic loop drive the main gains over the vanilla query-conditioned baseline. Code and implementation details are publicly available at https://github.com/bhosalems/CRAFT.

item.title: Decoupling Ego-Motion from Target Dynamics via Dual-Interval Motion Cues for UAV Detection
item.content:encoded: Object detection from Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) is challenged by severe ego-motion, camera jitter, and large scale variations. While modern detectors perform well on static images, their direct application to UAV video often fails, particularly for small objects in dynamic scenes. Existing motion-based methods either rely on computationally expensive optical flow or use single-interval differencing, which is sensitive to jitter and limited in capturing diverse motion patterns. We propose a vision-only motion-guided detection framework that decouples target motion from camera-induced disturbances. A homography-based Global Motion Compensation (GMC) first aligns adjacent frames. We then introduce a Dual-Interval Motion Extraction strategy that captures both short-term and long-term motion cues. To integrate these cues, a lightweight Motion-Guided Attention (MGA) module enhances feature representations within a Feature Pyramid Network. Experiments on the VisDrone-VID dataset demonstrate consistent improvements over a strong YOLOv8 baseline under severe ego-motion. Ablation studies further confirm the effectiveness of the dual-interval design and the proposed motion-guided attention mechanism.

item.title: Spectral Souping: A Unified Framework for Online Preference Alignment
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) effectively aligns Large Language Models (LLMs) with aggregate human preferences but often fails to address the diverse and conflicting needs of individual users. To overcome this issue, we introduce Spectral Souping, a unified framework for efficient, online preference alignment. Our contribution is the discovery of a universal spectral representation within LLMs, which is proven to be highly amenable to model merging. This theoretical insight enables a two-phase methodology: we first learn a basis of specialized policies offline, each focused on a distinct, fine-grained preference dimension. An online adaptation algorithm then efficiently ``soups'' these policies at inference time, either by merging their outputs or parameters, enabling rapid model adaptation without the need for costly online retraining w.r.t. tailored preference rewards. Experiments on online preference alignment benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves significant performance improvements over existing state-of-the-art approaches, presenting a scalable and computationally efficient solution for dynamically adapting LLMs to individual user preferences.

item.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
item.content:encoded: Temporal knowledge-graph data marketplaces face three coupled failures in static designs: stale hybrid index shortcuts reduce recall as edges evolve, stationary Shapley pricing misattributes value after distribution shifts, and uncoordinated agents over-consume a shared differential-privacy budget. We present CHRONOS, a three-layer architecture providing a unified treatment of these challenges with explicit public and private separation. Layer one applies neural-ODE temporal decay to shortcut edges, providing a per-query expected recall-loss bound of Big-O of Pq lambda delta t, with a monotone-envelope guarantee reducing bound looseness to 1.8 to 3.2 times observed loss. Layer two conditions Shapley valuation on detected changepoints and provides finite-sample error guarantees under noise. Layer three uses EXP3-IX to achieve Big-O of the square root of T log T regret while enforcing epsilon and delta differential privacy via moments accounting. CHRONOS releases a privatized affinity matrix per epoch using the Gaussian mechanism; all retrieval and ranking are post-processing, incurring no extra privacy cost. We provide multi-epoch settlement, scalability analysis for 500 sellers, and comparisons against accelerated baselines. Across four benchmarks, CHRONOS shows 0.937 recall at ten, 2.74 queries per second, 161 ms latency, and total epsilon of 4.25 at delta of 10 to the power of negative 6 under zCDP composition. These results indicate a competitive operating point. A limitation is that at this privacy level, released valuations remain noise-dominated; utility derives primarily from public index routing and adaptive scheduling driven by low-sensitivity statistics.

item.title: Sibyl-AutoResearch: Autonomous Research Needs Self-Evolving Trial-and-Error Harnesses, Not Paper Generators
item.content:encoded: Autonomous research systems increasingly make the scientific workflow executable: agents can propose ideas, run code, inspect results, and draft papers. But executable workflows do not by themselves produce research judgment. We analyze where current systems lose trial experience: weak evidence becomes prose, pilot signals become broad claims, memory remains textual, and recurring process failures do not change later behavior. We introduce Sibyl-AutoResearch, a self-evolving AutoResearch framework built around Scientific Trial-and-Error Harnesses. A harness lets agents run bounded trials, preserve positive and negative outcomes, and route lessons into later planning, validation, claim scope, scheduling, critique, writing, and harness repair. We formalize this through two auditable conversion units: trial-to-behavior conversion, which links trial signals to later research actions, and trial-to-harness-behavior conversion, which links recurring process failures to system updates. We implement the framework in SIBYL, a file-backed autonomous research system that exposes the state, roles, memory, gates, and artifact traces needed to inspect these conversion paths. A retrospective audit identifies eight high-confidence conversion events, with a median latency of one iteration and a maximum latency of three iterations. A recovered-failure registry further shows how five naturally occurring failure classes, including duplicate results, stale numbers, and unsupported statistics, were blocked, downgraded, or routed into later repair. These traces do not establish a comparative performance claim; they show that the proposed conversion units are recoverable from realistic autonomous-research workspaces. The SIBYL framework and system are available at https://github.com/Sibyl-Research-Team/AutoResearch-SibylSystem.

item.title: Preisach Attention: A Hysteretic Model of Sequential Memory
item.content:encoded: We introduce the Preisach Attention Layer (PAL), a novel sequence modelling architecture grounded in the classical Preisach hysteresis operator from mathematical physics. PAL replaces the softmax attention mechanism with a binary relay operator parameterised by learned activation and deactivation thresholds, maintaining a stack of local extrema as its internal state. A single-layer PAL-Transformer with O(1) depth is Turing-complete under arbitrary precision arithmetic, achievable through simulation of a two-stack pushdown automaton -- in contrast to the O(log n) depth required by standard hard-attention transformers. Second, we prove that the function classes computable by PAL and by the transformer are incomparable: PAL computes historical range statistics in O(1) layers that require O(log n) layers for transformers, while transformers support random-access retrieval that PAL cannot perform without auxiliary state. The separating property is rate-independence -- PAL responds only to the sequence of local extrema, not to absolute token positions or temporal spacing. Third, we show that the extremum stack constitutes a minimal sufficient statistic of the input history for all rate-independent functionals, providing a formal analogue of the wiping property in classical hysteresis theory. PAL is thus an efficient architecture for tasks with long episodic memory and weak positional dependence, with O(n log n) total inference cost versus O(n^2) for standard attention.

item.title: MTR-Suite: A Framework for Evaluating and Synthesizing Conversational Retrieval Benchmarks
item.content:encoded: Accurate evaluation of conversational retrieval is pivotal for advancing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. However, existing conversational retrieval benchmarks suffer from costly, sparse human annotation or rigid, unnatural automated heuristics. To address these challenges, we introduce MTR-Suite, a unified framework for auditing, synthesizing, and benchmarking retrieval. It features: (1) MTR-Eval, an LLM-based auditor quantifying alignment gaps in previous benchmarks; (2) MTR-Pipeline, a multi-agent system using greedy traversal clustering to generate high-fidelity dialogues at 1/400th human cost; and (3) MTR-Bench, a rigorous general-domain benchmark. MTR-Bench mimics production-style challenges (hard topic switching, verbosity), offering superior discriminative power. We make our code and data publicly available to facilitate future research at https://github.com/rangehow/mtr-suite.

item.title: General Preference Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: Post-training has split large language model (LLM) alignment into two largely disconnected tracks. Online reinforcement learning (RL) with verifiable rewards drives emergent reasoning on math and code but depends on a programmatic verifier that cannot reach open-ended tasks, while preference optimization handles open-ended generation yet forgoes the continuous exploration that powers online RL. Closing this gap requires a verifier for open-ended quality, but a scalar reward model is the wrong shape for the job. Quality is multi-dimensional, and any scalar score is an incomplete proxy that lets online RL collapse onto whichever axis the score is most sensitive to. We turn instead to the General Preference Model (GPM), which embeds responses into $k$ skew-symmetric subspaces and represents preference as a structured, intransitivity-aware comparison. Building on this, we propose General Preference Reinforcement Learning (GPRL), which carries the $k$-way structure through to the policy update. GPRL computes per-dimension group-relative advantages, normalizes each on its own scale so no axis can dominate, and aggregates them with context-dependent eigenvalues. The same structure powers a closed-loop drift monitor that detects single-axis exploitation and corrects it on the fly by reweighting dimensions and tightening the trust region. Starting from $\texttt{Llama-3-8B-Instruct}$, GPRL reaches a length-controlled win rate of $56.51\%$ on AlpacaEval~2.0 while also outperforming SimPO and SPPO on Arena-Hard, MT-Bench, and WildBench by resisting reward hacking across extended training runs.

item.title: Heartbeat-Bound Hierarchical Credentials: Cryptographic Revocation for AI Agent Swarms
item.content:encoded: Autonomous AI agents that spawn sub-agent swarms create a safety gap: existing credential revocation mechanisms, OAuth~2.0 introspection, OCSP, and W3C Status Lists, require network connectivity to a central authority, leaving ``zombie agents'' executing privileged operations for minutes to hours after operator shutdown. We present Heartbeat-Bound Hierarchical Credentials (HBHC), a cryptographic protocol that binds credential validity to periodic parent liveness proofs. Verifiers enforce freshness using only a cached public key and local clock; no network round-trip is required. When heartbeat generation ceases, all descendant credentials become unusable within a deterministically bounded window $W_z \le W_{\max} + Δ_h + ε$, conditional on bounded clock skew and parent keys held in secure enclaves. Evaluation at the protocol layer and with real LLM-backed agent swarms (GPT-4o-mini) demonstrates a 90$\times$ reduction in the zombie window over OAuth~2.0, 0.26~ms full authentication in Rust, 18,000+ verifications per second under concurrent HTTP load, and stable per-verification latency from 10 to 10,000 agents. Real-agent experiments show 0.71\% end-to-end overhead on tool calls, zero post-revocation tool calls under prompt injection that bypasses application-layer guardrails, and cascading revocation across a 49-agent four-level hierarchy within the theoretical bound.

item.title: UniVL: Unified Vision-Language Embedding for Spatially Grounded Contextual Image Generation
item.content:encoded: We introduce spatially grounded contextual image generation, a controllable image generation task that reframes the conditioning paradigm. Instead of supplying a reference image and a global text prompt through two separate encoders, one for vision and one for language, UniVL is trained to bind semantics to spatial locations directly from a single unified visual input, where the textual instruction is rendered onto the spatial mask. This removes the need for a standalone text encoder at inference time. The resulting model supports contextual image generation by following user-specified instructions about what should appear where, while substantially reducing computation. To address this task, we propose a framework in which the UniVL encoder, adapted from an optical-character-recognition-pretrained backbone, reads the unified condition optically and produces a UniVL embedding, fVIL, that fuses visual and semantic intent with spatial locations in a single token sequence. A two-stage pipeline first aligns UniVL with the VAE embedding space and then conditions a pretrained diffusion backbone entirely on UniVL embeddings, eliminating the standalone text encoder, such as T5. Although this reframing uses a deliberately minimal text interface, it yields strong empirical gains. On UniVL-ImgGen, a benchmark of 477K mask-annotated images that we construct for training and evaluation, UniVL improves image quality over text-prompted baselines, reducing FID from 14 to 11 and increasing PSNR from 16 to 20. It also eliminates the text encoder entirely, reducing inference TFLOPs by up to 52% and runtime by up to 44%. Additional ablation studies validate the contributions of the proposed components, paving the way for efficient, spatially grounded image generation with a unified conditioning paradigm.

item.title: Stage-adaptive Token Selection for Efficient Omni-modal LLMs
item.content:encoded: Omni-modal large language models (om-LLMs) achieve unified audio-visual understanding by encoding video and audio into temporally aligned token sequences interleaved at the window level. However, processing these dense non-textual tokens throughout the LLM incurs substantial computational overhead. Although training-free token selection can reduce this cost, existing methods either focus on visual-only inputs or prune om-LLM tokens only before the LLM with fixed per-modality ratios, failing to capture how cross-modal token importance evolves across layers. To address this limitation, we first analyze the layer-wise token dependency of om-LLMs. We find that visual and audio dependencies follow a block-wise pattern and gradually weaken with depth, indicating that many late-layer non-textual tokens become redundant after cross-modal fusion. Motivated by this observation, we propose SEATS, a training-free, stage-adaptive token selection method for efficient om-LLM inference. Before the LLM, SEATS removes spatiotemporal redundancy via attention-weighted diversity selection. Inside the LLM, it progressively prunes tokens across blocks and dynamically allocates the retention budget from temporal windows to modalities using query relevance scores. In late layers, it removes all remaining non-textual tokens once cross-modal fusion is complete. Experiments on Qwen2.5-Omni and Qwen3-Omni demonstrate that SEATS effectively improves inference efficiency. Retaining only 10% of visual and audio tokens, it achieves a 9.3x FLOPs reduction and a 4.8x prefill speedup while preserving 96.3% of the original performance.

item.title: Language-Switching Triggers Take a Latent Detour Through Language Models
item.content:encoded: Backdoor attacks on language models pose a growing security concern, yet the internal mechanisms by which a trigger sequence hijacks model computations remain poorly understood. We identify a circuit underlying a language-switching backdoor in an 8B-parameter autoregressive language model, where a three-word Latin trigger (nine tokens) redirects English output to French. We decompose the circuit into three phases: (1) distributed attention heads at early layers compose the trigger tokens into the last sequence position; (2) the resulting signal propagates through mid-layers in a subspace orthogonal to the model's natural language-identity direction; (3) the MLP at the final layer converts this latent signal into French logits. The entire circuit flows through a serial bottleneck at a single position: corrupting that position at any layer entirely mitigate the trigger but also hinder the model's capabilities. The orthogonal latent encoding suggests that defenses that search for language-like signals in intermediate representations would miss this trigger entirely.

item.title: LambdaPO: A Lambda Style Policy Optimization for Reasoning Language Models
item.content:encoded: Group Relative Policy Optimization(GRPO) has become a cornerstone of modern reinforcement learning alignment, prized for its efficacy in foregoing an explicit value-critic by leveraging reward normalization across sampled trajectory cohorts. However, the method's reliance on a monolithic statistical baseline, such as the group mean, collapses the relational topology of the trajectory space into a single scalar, thereby erasing the fine-grained preference information essential for navigating complex, rank-sensitive reward landscapes. To address this issue, we introduce a novel framework, Lambda Policy Optimization (LambdaPO), that addresses this information-theoretic bottleneck by re-conceptualizing advantage estimation from a scalar value to a decomposed, pairwise preference structure. Specifically, the advantage for any given trajectory is formulated as the integrated sum of reward differentials against all peers in its cohort, where each pairwise comparison is dynamically attenuated by the policy's own probabilistic confidence in the established preference. To further mitigate the sparsity of binary outcome supervision, we augment the objective with a semantic density reward, derived from the precision-recall alignment between generated reasoning traces and ground-truth solutions. As a result, our method can mine more fine-grained optimization signals from a group of rollouts, guiding the LLM to a better optima. Experimental results across challenging math reasoning and question-answering tasks demonstrates that LambdaPO improves performance compared to the baseline methods.

item.title: MaSC: A Masked Similarity Metric for Evaluating Concept-Driven Generation
item.content:encoded: Evaluating single-concept personalization in text-to-image diffusion requires measuring both concept preservation, which captures identity fidelity to a reference, and prompt following, which captures whether the generated scene matches the prompt. Existing metrics commonly compute these signals using global image or text-image embeddings, such as CLIP-I, DINO, and CLIP-T. We show that such metrics correlate poorly with human perception because they attend to the image as a whole instead of separating the concept subject from the background. We introduce MaSC, a masked similarity metric that uses externally provided foreground concept masks to decompose evaluation into subject-specific concept preservation and background-based prompt following. MaSC computes both scores from frozen SigLIP2 SO400M-NaFlex features: concept preservation is measured by masked max-cosine matching between foreground reference patches and generated-image patches, while prompt following is measured by comparing a background-only pooled image embedding to a subject-stripped prompt embedding. On DreamBench++ human ratings, MaSC achieves Krippendorff alpha = 0.471 for concept preservation, outperforming all tested non-LLM baselines and GPT-4V, and approaching GPT-4o. On ORIDa, a real-photo identity-preservation benchmark across physical environments, MaSC achieves AUC = 0.992, nearly perfectly distinguishing same-subject from cross-subject pairs. Its prompt-following score also outperforms the CLIP-T baseline shipped with DreamBench++. These results show that spatially decomposed aggregation is a strong design principle for evaluating concept-driven generation.

item.title: When the Majority Votes Wrong, the Intervention Timing for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning Hides in the Extinction Window
item.content:encoded: Test-time reinforcement learning (TTRL) reports substantial accuracy gains on mathematical reasoning benchmarks using majority vote as a pseudo-label signal. We argue these gains are systematically misinterpreted: most reflect sharpening of already-solvable problems rather than genuine learning, while problems corrupted from correct to incorrect outnumber truly learned ones, and this damage is irreversible once majority vote locks onto a wrong answer. Per-problem tracking reveals that correct-answer signals in low-ability problems are briefly active before being permanently suppressed, a phenomenon we term the \textit{Correct-Answer Extinction Window}, with Flip Rate (FR) as its leading indicator. We thus propose \textbf{TTRL-Guard}, a lightweight framework with three mechanisms targeting the extinction window: Flip-Rate-Aware Reward Scaling (FRS) down-weights at-risk updates as FR declines, Minority-Preserving Sampling (MPS) retains gradient signal from minority correct answers, and Risk-Conditioned Sparse Updatings (RCSU) suspends updates on polarized problems. Experiments across three models and four benchmarks show that TTRL-Guard achieves the best average pass@1 on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct and Qwen3-4B, improves relatively over TTRL by +54\% on AIME 2025. \footnote{Our code and implementation details are available at https://github.com/linhxkkkk/TTRL-Guard.

item.title: 基于LLM的反思式提示调优方法RPT
item.content:encoded:
RPT通过模拟人类工程师流程提升提示优化效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Reflective Prompt Tuning (RPT) 的框架，利用大语言模型的功能调用模拟人类提示工程师的迭代工作流程。该方法通过诊断函数评估目标模型在整个优化集上的表现，总结重复出现的失败模式，并生成结构化诊断报告。优化器结合历史报告对提示进行迭代修改，同时支持基于置信度校准信号的优化。实验表明，RPT在三项推理任务中提升了初始提示性能，尤其在多跳和数学推理任务中效果显著，同时改善了置信度校准。

item.title: 一种无需训练的长视频生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需训练的长视频生成方法，提升视频时序一致性与质量。



摘要： 本文针对视频扩散模型在长序列生成中的挑战，提出一种无需额外训练的推理阶段方法。该方法通过重叠滑动窗口和Tweedie匹配技术，确保不同窗口间的流形约束与时间一致性，并结合随机早期采样策略，在高噪声阶段注入新噪声以同步各窗口轨迹，最终过渡到确定性ODE采样以保持视觉细节。该方法适用于多种视频生成模型，显著延长生成视频长度，优于现有训练-free 和自回归基线，在时序一致性和视觉质量上表现更优，还可扩展至音视频联合生成和文本到3DGS任务。

item.title: 基于神经稀疏检索的音乐搜索系统优化
item.content:encoded:
提升音乐搜索效率与准确性的神经稀疏检索系统。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种针对大规模音乐搜索场景的鲁棒神经稀疏检索系统，旨在解决查询与索引元数据之间的偏差问题。该系统采用无推理的稀疏检索架构，并结合领域特定的细粒度子词分词策略，通过限制短字符长度来增强表面形式的鲁棒性。离线预计算神经嵌入和词项扩展，使在线处理仅需最小化分词和IDF加权，实现零延迟开销。实验结果显示，该系统在600万文档的生产语料库中达到91.4%的召回率，显著优于传统n-gram方法，同时提升了探索效率。

item.title: 量子机器学习在无人机异常检测中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
量子机器学习在无人机异常检测中表现出潜在优势。



摘要： 本文研究了量子机器学习在无人机（UAV）异常检测中的应用，通过多传感器数据集TLM:UAV进行无泄漏评估。研究提出三种贡献：(i) 一种基于时间块的分组协议以提高评估准确性；(ii) 三种模式的特征审计，分析物理信号与上下文代理信息对准确性的贡献；(iii) 一种XGBoost与数据重上传（DRU）混合分类器，在相同预算下与其他非线性模型对比。结果表明，训练后的DRU混合模型在严格模式下F1宏值有所提升，并具有最低误报率。研究将此视为量子增强的渐进性优势，并提供了一个开放的Qiskit 2.x实现作为NISQ时代航空系统安全分析的基准。

item.title: 优化感知的前馈3D高斯点云重建方法ForeSplat
item.content:encoded:
ForeSplat提升3DGS重建质量，实现快速高效优化。



摘要： 本文提出ForeSplat，一种优化感知的训练框架，用于前馈3D高斯点云（3DGS）模型。该方法通过设计初始化以适应后续优化，减少模型容量压力，从而在紧凑网络下实现高质量重建。核心是MetaGrad，一种轻量级多锚点元梯度规则，通过短内循环优化轨迹采样并反向传播梯度，无需额外推理成本即可在短时间内达到高精度重建。实验表明，ForeSplat在多种架构中均优于传统方法，显著缩短优化步骤并提升重建质量。

item.title: NaviEdit：解耦编辑进度与模型规模的图像编辑方法
item.content:encoded:
NaviEdit通过解耦编辑进度与模型规模提升图像编辑性能。



摘要： 文章指出，尽管扩散和流模型在生成方面表现出色，但真实图像编辑仍面临语义可编辑性与结构保真度之间的权衡。这种限制源于现有方法中编辑进度与模型规模的隐式耦合，导致强编辑需要访问更嘈杂的状态，从而浪费计算资源。为此，作者提出NaviEdit，一种无需训练的推理时控制器，通过严格的自我一致性协议解耦编辑进度与模型规模。NaviEdit在滚动级别运行，不改变预训练模型，将固定步数预算重新分配到语义响应的中间尺度，而非破坏性的高噪声区域。实验表明该方法在多种编辑器和流模型上均取得正向提升，验证了解耦作为可移植推理控制原则的有效性。

item.title: 医疗AI中的价值多元性审计框架
item.content:encoded:
研究提出医疗AI价值多元性审计框架，揭示模型决策与医生伦理分歧。



摘要： 文章探讨了医疗AI在伦理价值上的多元性问题，指出尽管医生在临床实践中会考虑患者价值观并平衡不同伦理原则，但当前大型语言模型的决策往往缺乏这种多样性。研究提出了一种审计框架，包括临床验证的伦理困境基准和从决策中恢复价值优先级的方法。结果显示，虽然模型在推理中表现出价值冲突讨论（Overton多元主义），但其决策在重复采样和语义变化下高度一致，未能反映医生群体的分布性多元性。部分模型对患者自主权的权重过低，可能在大规模应用中加剧伦理偏见，建议通过多模型协作来平衡伦理视角。

item.title: SGD性能提升：通过学习率剪裁实现与Adam相当的预训练效果
item.content:encoded:
SGD通过学习率剪裁可显著提升性能，接近Adam效果。



摘要： 本文研究了SGD在预训练大语言模型时性能不如Adam的原因，发现SGD难以维持与Adam相近的大有效学习率。通过分析训练动态，作者指出梯度大小和权重-梯度比值的变化是关键因素，尤其在大规模批量训练中更为明显。此外，输出层梯度在不同词符上分布不均，导致SGD的学习率受限。基于此，作者提出简单的学习率剪裁机制，使SGD在大规模实验中性能大幅提升，验证集损失差距从50%缩小至3.5%。

item.title: 面向5G核心的层级多智能体自主网络架构研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型多智能体架构提升网络自主性与故障恢复能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种分层多智能体参考架构，以实现Level 4/5级别的自主网络。该架构通过双驱动协调器协调专用执行智能体，并利用共享公共记忆实现统一领域知识管理。关键创新在于引入智能体自我意识，使系统能够兼顾战略治理与快速故障恢复。在5G核心环境中进行实例化验证，结果表明该系统在拥塞情况下仍能保持关键吞吐量，并将平均修复时间减少86%，验证了其在战略规划与操作韧性方面的有效性。

item.title: 提升多模态大语言模型跨视角推理能力的CrossView Suite
item.content:encoded:
CrossView Suite提升MLLM跨视角推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出CrossView Suite，包括CrossViewSet、CrossViewBench和CrossViewer三个组件，以解决多模态大语言模型（MLLM）在跨视角推理中的三大瓶颈：数据不足、评估缺失和对齐机制缺乏。CrossViewSet是一个包含1.6M样本的高质量跨视角指令数据集，覆盖17种任务类型；CrossViewBench用于系统评估MLLM的跨视角空间理解能力；CrossViewer则是一种分阶段的跨视角推理框架，通过感知、对齐和推理流程提升模型性能。实验表明，大规模数据、系统评估和显式对齐对推动MLLM实现真实世界的空间智能至关重要。

item.title: 基于深度学习的医学图像分割在新冠CT影像中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
深度学习在新冠CT影像分割中表现优异，F1分数达98%。



摘要： 近年来，深度学习在医学图像分割领域的关注度显著提升，但因缺乏统一的性能评估方法和数据集差异，其可靠性受到限制。本研究旨在全面评估先进的分割框架与预训练模型在新冠CT影像中检测病灶的能力，并为其他影像分割任务提供参考。研究整合了Unet、PSPNet、Linknet和FPN四种架构，搭配VGG 19、DenseNet 121等六种预训练编码器，进行了二分类和多分类实验。分析结果显示，深度学习模型在三个新冠CT数据集上均表现出高精度，其中二分类的F1分数最高达到98%，多分类分别为75%和77%。人工智能和深度学习技术在疫情诊断中具有重要价值。

item.title: 面向第一人称视频理解的细粒度推理基准EgoCoT-Bench
item.content:encoded:
提出EgoCoT-Bench，用于评估第一人称视频中的细粒度操作推理能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了EgoCoT-Bench，一个用于评估多模态大语言模型在第一人称视频中进行细粒度操作推理能力的基准。该基准包含3,172个可验证的问题对，覆盖4个任务组和12个子任务，涵盖感知、回溯、预测和高层推理。EgoCoT-Bench通过时空场景图生成框架构建，并由人工标注确保准确性和细粒度质量。实验表明，现有模型在细粒度推理方面仍面临挑战，且许多模型的解释与答案不一致。项目页面和补充材料已公开。

item.title: 基于轨迹控制的模型安全防护框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于轨迹控制的模型安全防护框架，提升交互安全性。



摘要： 文章探讨了在教育、心理健康和护理等敏感领域中，基础模型的安全性问题。现有防护方法多为经验性风险降低，未能提供可执行的行为保障。本文从机器人学中借鉴思想，将防护机制重新定义为对交互轨迹的实时行为控制，并提出了Grounded Observer框架。该框架在小对话、自闭症治疗和学校行为干预等实际场景中进行了验证，能够有效防止交互偏离安全范围，同时适应不同社会情境。文章还讨论了框架的扩展方向和未来研究路径。

item.title: 基于LIFT与PLACE的扩散模型知识蒸馏方法
item.content:encoded:
提出LIFT与PLACE框架提升扩散模型知识蒸馏效果。



摘要： 本文研究了扩散模型知识蒸馏中，由于教师模型复杂的去噪过程导致学生模型难以模仿的问题。为此，提出了LIFT和PLACE框架。LIFT通过粗到细的策略分解任务，先进行粗略对齐再进行精细优化；PLACE则通过分组处理空间非均匀误差，提供局部自适应指导。实验表明，该方法在多种扩散模型、任务和数据集上均有效，尤其在极端压缩情况下表现优异，FID得分显著优于传统方法。

item.title: COAgents：一种基于多智能体的车辆路径问题求解框架
item.content:encoded:
COAgents通过多智能体协作提升VRP求解效率与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为COAgents的协同多智能体框架，用于解决车辆路径问题（VRP）。该框架将搜索过程建模为图结构，其中节点代表解决方案，边表示局部优化或大规模扰动。通过动态构建部分搜索图（PSG），COAgents训练了节点选择代理、移动选择代理和跳跃代理，分别用于强化搜索和触发探索。相比端到端学习方法，COAgents将通用搜索控制与领域特定编码分离，提高了任务适应性。实验表明，COAgents在CVRP和VRPTW基准测试中表现优异，特别是在VRPTW上超越现有学习方法，显著缩小了与最优解的差距。

item.title: SciCustom：面向科学应用的LLM基准测试框架
item.content:encoded:
SciCustom框架提升科学领域LLM评估精度与适用性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为SciCustom的新框架，用于构建针对特定科学应用场景的基准测试。该框架通过将科学知识组织为结构化的知识单元，并利用多模型共识识别相关知识，从而实现高效、精准的基准生成。实验表明，SciCustom能揭示标准基准未捕捉到的LLM科学能力差异，且无需专家标注或合成问题生成，具有良好的可扩展性和应用适配性。

item.title: 基于知识蒸馏的高效欺诈评分模型研究
item.content:encoded:
通过知识蒸馏提升欺诈评分模型效率，实现CPU上快速推理。



摘要： 本文提出一种方法，将高性能但耗时的表格基础模型（TFM）通过知识蒸馏转化为高效的XGBoost或CatBoost模型，使其在CPU上运行时间低于2毫秒。核心挑战在于教师模型在上下文学习中泄露标签，导致软目标失去类间结构。通过分层交叉验证的教师标签方法解决此问题。实验表明，在153个数据集上，蒸馏后的模型达到教师模型96.5%的AUC，且比调优后的CatBoost表现更优。研究还发现，教师排名可准确转移到学生模型，低维数据收益更大，多教师平均对MLP有益但对树模型效果有限。该方法已开源。

item.title: 可解释AI中用户信任校准的实验研究
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨了XAI系统中用户信任与模型能力的匹配问题。



摘要： 本文通过一项在线实验研究，分析了可解释AI（XAI）中用户对模型的信任判断与实际性能之间的关系。研究采用418名英国代表性样本，参与者通过固定的XAI界面（包括恶性程度评分、可靠性评分和显著性图）评估皮肤病变案例。实验操纵了五种不同的引导条件，并分析了用户信任判断（TAIS和个案评分）与客观性能基准之间的偏差。结果显示，仅限于个案的限制披露能有效影响信任校准，而短期经验并未带来逐步校准。此外，实际观察到的预测质量比实验条件更能解释信任变化。研究还指出用户在感知信任、可信度和准确性估计之间存在混淆，强调了在XAI评估中设计限制沟通的重要性。

item.title: 提升文档篡改检测鲁棒性的JPEG量化表研究
item.content:encoded:
真实量化表训练提升文档篡改定位效果。



摘要： 本文研究了文档篡改检测模型在实际工作流中表现不佳的原因，发现主要源于训练时使用的JPEG量化表分布过于单一。通过对比不同架构在标准量化表和真实操作环境量化表下的表现，发现显式引入量化表信息的模型在真实场景中表现更优。实验表明，使用真实量化表进行训练可显著提升检测性能并降低误报率，研究成果已开源。

item.title: 文学文本机器翻译中隐喻的后编辑行为研究
item.content:encoded:
研究显示，三分之一的隐喻被后编辑者修改，表明文学MT存在挑战。



摘要： 本文探讨了文学文本的后编辑者如何应对神经机器翻译和大语言模型生成的隐喻翻译。研究发现，约三分之一的隐喻在输出中被后编辑者修改，反映出文学机器翻译在比喻语言处理上的困难。后编辑者普遍意识到过于直译的问题，尤其是在多词表达方面。有时他们难以判断解决方案是否合适。他们对机器翻译质量评价较低，并认为后编辑比从头翻译更费力，这支持了先前研究的观点，即后编辑限制了译者的创造力并削弱了其对文本的归属感。

item.title: DPO与RLHF的理论等价性分析及CPO方法提出
item.content:encoded:
DPO并非普遍等同于RLHF，CPO实现更可靠的对齐。



摘要： 本文研究了直接偏好优化（DPO）与基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）之间的理论等价性，发现这种等价性是条件性的，依赖于一个在实践中常被违反的假设：RLHF最优策略必须偏好人类偏好的响应。当该假设不成立时，DPO会优化相对于参考策略的优势，而非绝对对齐人类偏好，导致策略在降低DPO损失的同时选择不受欢迎的响应。文章揭示了该假设失效的情况，证明DPO和RLHF在此情况下优化的是不同目标。为解决此问题，作者提出了约束偏好优化（CPO），通过引入约束实现可证明的对齐。此外，文章还通过软间隔排序提供了几何解释，指出DPO可能采用负目标进行间隔排序。实验表明，CPO在标准基准上表现优异。

item.title: 基于显式遮挡关系的图像生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出OcclusionFormer模型解决图像生成中的遮挡问题。



摘要： 近年来，布局到图像生成模型在空间控制方面取得了显著进展，但仍面临对象间遮挡的问题。现有方法在边界框重叠时缺乏明确的遮挡信息，导致重叠区域生成模糊，影响复杂遮挡关系的确定。为此，研究者构建了SA-Z数据集，包含显式的遮挡顺序和像素级标注，并提出OcclusionFormer框架，通过解耦实例并使用体渲染进行合成，显式建模Z轴优先级。同时引入查询对齐损失以提升空间精度和语义一致性。该方法有效减少了重叠区域的模糊性，确保正确的遮挡依赖关系，提升了多种场景下的生成准确性。

item.title: Ada2MS：融合AdamW与动量SGD的优化算法
item.content:encoded:
Ada2MS结合AdamW与动量SGD优势，提升模型训练效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种新的优化算法Ada2MS，通过在元素级二阶矩估计和全局二阶矩估计之间进行连续指数插值，实现AdamW与动量SGD行为的平滑过渡。该方法旨在平衡两者的优势，提高模型的泛化能力。实验表明，在视觉任务中，Ada2MS在统一优化器比较协议下表现优异。相关代码已开源。

item.title: 实时手术流程理解的流式视觉-语言模型SurgOnAir
item.content:encoded:
SurgOnAir实现手术流程的实时理解与响应。



摘要： 本文提出SurgOnAir，一种流式视觉-语言模型，用于实时理解手术流程。该模型通过逐帧处理并生成叙述性文本，实现实时响应手术动态变化。基于自建的SurgOnAir-11k数据集，模型能够生成多层次的文本响应，并通过特殊过渡标记识别关键流程变化。实验表明，SurgOnAir在统一多个层次的手术流程理解方面表现优异，为智能手术辅助提供了新方法。代码和数据集将公开。

item.title: 基于长期训练历史的自适应评分奖励机制AMARIS
item.content:encoded:
AMARIS通过长期评估记忆提升奖励塑造效果。



摘要： 本文提出AMARIS，一种基于长期训练历史的自适应评分奖励机制。传统方法在每次训练步骤中使用局部信号调整评分标准，但无法积累和复用评估知识，限制了模型检测重复低效行为的能力。AMARIS通过分析每个训练轨迹，将结果汇总并从持久化评估记忆中检索相关上下文，动态更新评分标准，从而提升奖励信号的质量。实验表明，AMARIS在封闭和开放领域均优于基线，且仅增加约5%的运行时间开销。该方法将评分奖励塑造从一次性策略转变为数据驱动的持续优化过程。

item.title: 提升文本到图像生成创造力的自创扩散模型
item.content:encoded:
提出SCDiff模型提升T2I生成的创意性与艺术价值。



摘要： 本文针对文本到图像生成中创造力不足的问题，提出Self-Creative Diffusion (SCDiff) 模型。该模型包含两个核心模块：可学习空间加权（LSW）模块和视觉-语义混合损失（VSML）。LSW通过参数化Kaiser-Bessel窗口增强图像中心特征，促进新颖性和惊喜感；VSML引入双损失函数，确保生成图像在语义上与文本一致，同时在视觉上与原图有显著差异，从而提升创意性和视觉连贯性。实验表明，该模型在创造性、语义对齐和视觉一致性方面均有显著提升。

item.title: 医疗大语言模型在非指南场景下的推理评估
item.content:encoded:
评估医疗大模型在非指南场景下的推理能力，提升真实临床应用可靠性。



摘要： 本文介绍了OGCaReBench，一个专注于评估医疗大语言模型（LLMs）在非典型指南场景下临床推理能力的基准测试。该基准基于医学病例报告构建，包含需要自由文本回答的长格式临床问题。实验表明，即使最先进的模型（如GPT-5.2）仅能正确回答56%的问题，而通过检索医学文献可将准确率提升至82%。这凸显了证据基础推理在真实医疗任务中的重要性，并为提升通用和医疗大模型的可靠性提供了基础。

item.title: 基于部件感知控制的3D形状合成与编辑方法CompoSE
item.content:encoded:
CompoSE实现3D形状的部件级精确编辑与合成。



摘要： 本文提出CompoSE，一种基于部件感知控制的3D形状合成与编辑方法。该方法通过输入粗略几何体素（如边界框）来生成可局部编辑的3D对象。其核心在于使用扩散Transformer架构，在局部处理和全局上下文聚合之间交替，并引入新的条件技术以确保对用户输入的强遵循。CompoSE能够从用户的粗略布局中直接推断部件语义和对称性，无需部件级文本提示。实验表明，该方法在引导合成任务中显著优于现有方法。

item.title: CAdam：解决生成蒸馏中密度膨胀问题的新方法
item.content:encoded:
CAdam提升3DGS密度控制效率，减少冗余点云。



摘要： 本文针对3D高斯溅射（3DGS）在基于优化的生成蒸馏框架中出现的密度膨胀问题进行研究。传统基于幅度的密度增加机制因生成引导的随机性而失效，导致冗余点云过多。为此，作者提出Context-Adaptive Moment Estimation（CAdam），将密度增加重新定义为统计信号验证问题。CAdam利用梯度的一阶矩，通过干扰原理区分几何信号与噪声，实现更高效的密度控制。结合分位数上下文感知和信噪比门控机制，CAdam在多个任务中将高斯点数量减少85%-97%，同时保持高质量的视觉效果，显著提升了生成蒸馏的内存效率。

item.title: 文本数据在气候灾害社会经济影响分析中的应用与挑战
item.content:encoded:
文本数据用于分析气候灾害的社会经济影响，需解决方法论复杂性问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了自然语言处理和大语言模型在气候灾害社会经济影响分析中的应用，指出当前研究在定义影响、处理时空偏差及建模策略上缺乏统一标准，导致研究透明度和可比性不足。文章总结了常见实践，分析了关键挑战，并提出了改进建议，旨在推动构建更准确的文本衍生社会经济影响数据集，以支持灾害风险管理和归因研究。
